queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves Windows Event Logs with IDs 4756, 4728 and 4732 indicating that a security group membership has been updated. Adversaries quite often will attempt to add a compromised unprivileged user account to a privileged domain group to escalate privileges. Such events should be investigated especially if a highly privileged domain group membership like "Enterprise Admins" or "Backup Operators" has been updated.

osQuery.label: windows_eventlogs_security_group_membership_updated
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
datetime,
regex_match(data, 'MemberName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS member_name,
regex_match(data, 'TargetUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS target_user_name,
regex_match(data, 'TargetDomainName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS target_domain_name,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_user_name,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectDomainName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_domain_name, 
data
FROM
windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid in (4732, 4728, 4756) AND
target_user_name LIKE regex_match(target_user_name, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "target_user_name_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Windows Event Logs Security Group Membership Updated

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It will show history of shutdown or restart events.

osQuery.label: windows_shutdown_restart_history_monitoring
osQuery.name: Events
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
CASE
WHEN eventid = 6005 THEN "Restart" 
WHEN eventid = 6006 THEN "Shutdown" 
ELSE "Unknown"
END Event
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "System" AND
eventid in (6005, 6006);

queries.title: Windows Shutdown/Restart History Monitoring
warnings: This query retrieve data from Windows Event Log. If Event Log service has been disabled or data cleared, then this query will not show any results.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. Non-volatile Random Access Memory (NVRAM) is a small amount of RAM that stores certain settings. Shutting down the system does not clear NVRAM. The power source on the motherboard keeps it alive. Settings that NVRAM stores are: 
* sound volume
* display resolution
* startup dick selection
* time zone
* recent kernel panic information
This query retrieves NVRAM configuration data from the host.

osQuery.label: macos_nvram_monitoring
osQuery.name: NVRAM Settings
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, type, value 
FROM nvram 
WHERE 
name LIKE regex_match(name, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "name_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: NVRAM Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. The I/O Registry is a dynamic database that describes a collection of "live" objects (nubs or drivers) and tracks the provider-client relationships between them. When hardware is added or removed from the system, the Registry is immediately updated to reflect the new configuration of devices. A dynamic part of the I/O Kit, the Registry is not stored on disk or archived between boots. Instead, it is built at each system boot and resides in memory.
The Device Tree plane represents the Open Firmware device hierarchy. This query retrieves I/O Kit registry data related to the Device Tree plane:
* name - device node name
* class - best matching device class (most-specific category)
* id - IOKit internal registry ID
* parent - parent device registry ID
* device_path - device tree path
* service - 1 if the device conforms to IOService else 0
* busy_state - 1 if the device is in a busy state else 0
* retain_count - the device reference count 
* depth - device nested depth

osQuery.label: macos_iokit_devicetree_monitoring
osQuery.name: IOKit Registry Matching the DeviceTree Plane Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
name, class, id, parent, device_path,
CASE service WHEN "1" THEN "True" ELSE "False" END "Device Conforms to IOService",
CASE busy_state WHEN "1" THEN "True" ELSE "False" END "Device is in a Busy State", 
retain_count, depth 
FROM iokit_devicetree
WHERE
name LIKE regex_match(name, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "name_pattern"), 0) AND
class LIKE regex_match(class, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "class_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: IOKit Registry Matching the DeviceTree Plane Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. A user agent is a string that identifies the browser and the client operating system to the web server. Malicious actors may attempt to use user-agent strings from browsers to masquerade command-and-control traffic as legitimate web requests. This query monitors for powershell scripts that specify the user agent for web requests.

osQuery.label: user_agent_masquerade_attempt
osQuery.name: User Agent Masquerade Attempt
osQuery.sql:
SELECT datetime, time, script_block_count, script_block_id, script_name, script_path, script_text 
FROM orbital_powershell_events 
WHERE script_text LIKE "%headers.add%user-agent%" 
AND script_text LIKE "%mozilla%";

queries.title: User Agent Masquerade Attempt

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. WmiPrvSE is the host process for various WMI provider DLLs. It gets installed on the system when installing the OS and by default it is running from the "system32\wbem" or "syswow64\wbem" directories. Malware often attempts to disguise itself as a legitimate windows process and one way to detect it is to check for a correct image path for a suspicious executable.

osQuery.label: wmiprvse.exe_incorrect_default_location
osQuery.name: Wmiprvse.exe Incorrect Default Location
osQuery.sql:
SELECT p.name, p.path, p.pid, p.parent, p.cmdline 
FROM processes p 
WHERE p.name LIKE "wmiprvse.exe" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "%:\windows\system32\wbem\wmiprvse.exe" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "%:\windows\syswow64\wbem\wmiprvse.exe" 
AND p.path!="";

queries.title: Wmiprvse.exe Incorrect Default Location

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. This query returns data on the chrome extensions installed on the host for a particular user. This information can be used in attempt to detect an extension performing malicious activity since is a common practice for malware to disguise itself as a legitimate and well-known browser extension. Following data is retrieved:
* username - the local user that owns the extension
* name - display name 
* identifier 
* version
* description
* locale
* update_url - extension-supplied update URI
* persistent - 1 If extension is persistent across all tabs else 0
* path - path to extension folder

osQuery.label: chrome_extensions_monitoring
osQuery.name: Chrome Extensions

queries.title: Chrome Browser Extensions Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It is designed to monitor files in the Orbital installation directory. Following data is retrieved:
* path
* sha256
* user ID
* group ID
* file size 
* last time file was modified

osQuery.label: orbital_installation_directory
osQuery.name: Orbital Installation Files
osQuery.sql:
SELECT f.path, h.sha256, f.uid, f.gid, f.size, datetime(f.mtime,"unixepoch") as last_modification_time
FROM file f
JOIN hash h ON f.path=h.path
WHERE f.path LIKE "C:\Program Files\Cisco\Orbital\%%";

queries.title: Orbital Installation Directory Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It scans startup items binaries for suspicious strings indicating that a binary contains few imports and dynamic loading functions:
* path - the path scanned
* matches - list of YARA matches
* count - number of YARA matches
* strings - matching strings
* tags - matching tags
* sha256 of the scanned binary

osQuery.label: windows_yara_pe_dynamic_load
osQuery.name: YARA Scan Results
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
y.path, y.matches, y.count,
y.strings, y.tags,
h.sha256
FROM yara y
LEFT JOIN hash h ON h.path=y.path
WHERE
y.path IN (SELECT path FROM startup_items) AND
y.sigrule = 'import "pe" rule pe_dynamic_load : anomaly { condition: pe.imports("kernel32.dll","LoadLibraryA") and pe.imports("kernel32.dll","GetProcAddress") and pe.number_of_imports <= 10 }' AND
y.count != 0;

queries.title: YARA Scan: PE Contains Few Imports and Dynamic Loading Functions

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows and Linux. The Intel Management Engine (ME) is an embedded microcontroller (integrated on some Intel chipsets) running a lightweight microkernel operating system that provides a variety of features and services for Intel processor–based computer systems. It could be abused by the attackers for firmware-based attacks and it should be updated on a regular basis to prevent such attempts. This query retrieves Intel ME version from the host.

osQuery.label: windows_intel_me_info
osQuery.name: Intel ME/CSE Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
version
FROM intel_me_info
WHERE
version LIKE regex_match(version, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "version_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Windows Intel ME/CSE Version

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves following Event Logs from the system:
* 11000 - wireless network association started
* 8001 - successful connection to wireless network
* 8002 - failed connection to wireless network
* 8003 - disconnect from wireless network
* 6100 - network diagnostics
These event logs could be useful when investigating historical records of wireless connections on the host.

osQuery.label: windows_wlan_events_monitoring
osQuery.name: Windows WLAN Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime AS "TimeGenerated",
data AS Message,
eventid AS EventIdentifier,
provider_name AS SourceName,
channel AS Logfile,
"N/A" AS "TimeWritten",
"N/A" AS "Type"
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE (
channel = "Microsoft-Windows-WLAN-AutoConfig/Operational" AND
eventid IN (11000, 8001, 8002, 8003)) OR (
channel = "System" AND 
eventid = 6100);

queries.title: Windows WLAN Events Monitoring
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. The Image File Execution Options (IFEO) registry key is used to attach a debugger to an application when it starts. Malware can register itself as a debugger of a common application that starts early during windows boot process. This is means for establishing persistence.

osQuery.label: image_file_execution_options_monitoring
osQuery.name: IFEO Registry Keys
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key as reg_key, path, name, data, datetime(mtime,"unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE (
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\%%" OR
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432NODE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\%%") 
AND name LIKE "Debugger";

queries.title: Image File Execution Options Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves Windows Event Logs with ID "4776" that are generated by the system when an attempt to validate users credentials has been made.

osQuery.label: windows_eventlogs_credential_validation_attempt
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
regex_match(data, '.*PackageName":"([^"]+).*', 1) as package_name,
regex_match(data, '.*TargetUserName":"([^"]+).*', 1) as target_user_name,
regex_match(data, '.*Status":"([^"]+).*', 1) AS status,
case regex_match(data, '.*Status":"([^"]+).*', 1) 
when "0xc0000064" then "The username you typed does not exist. Bad username."
when "0xc000006a" then "Account logon with misspelled or bad password."
when "0xc000006d" then "Generic logon failure."
when "0xc000006f" then "Account logon outside authorized hours."
when "0xc0000070" then "Account logon from unauthorized workstation."
when "0xc0000071" then "Account logon with expired password."
when "0xc0000072" then "Account logon to account disabled by administrator."
when "0xc0000193" then "Account logon with expired account."
when "0xc0000224" then "Account logon with 'Change Password at Next Logon' flagged."
when "0xc0000234" then "Account logon with account locked."
when "0xc0000371" then "The local account store does not contain secret material for the specified account."
when "0x0" then "No Errors"
else "Unknown"
END status_description, 
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4776 AND 
status LIKE regex_match(status, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "status_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Credential Validation Events Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves Windows Event Logs with ID 1102 indicating that audit logs were cleared. This could indicate an attacker trying to evade detection on the endpoint:
* datetime - system time at which the event occurred
* subject_user_name - user who cleared the log 
* subject_domain_name - user domain of the user who cleared the log 
* data - data associated with the event

osQuery.label: windows_eventlogs_audit_log_cleared
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_user_name,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectDomainName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_domain_name,
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 1102;

queries.title: Windows Events Audit Log was Cleared

queries.description:
This queries is applicable to MacOS. It is designed to capture volatile and non-volatile data from the endpoint that can help with forensic investigations.
New queries:
Removed queries:
Updated queries:

osQuery.label: hosts_file
osQuery.name: Hosts File Data

osQuery.label: sha256_hash_of_running_processes
osQuery.name: SHA256 Hash Of Running Processes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT p.pid, p.name, p.path, p.cmdline, p.state,  h.sha256 
FROM processes p 
INNER JOIN hash h 
ON p.path=h.path;

osQuery.label: interface_names_and_associated_ips
osQuery.name: Interface Names And Associated IPs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT interface, address, mask, type, friendly_name 
FROM interface_addresses 
WHERE interface NOT LIKE "lo%" 
AND interface!=1;

osQuery.label: process_running_without_a_binary_on_disk
osQuery.name: Process Running Without A Binary On Disk

osQuery.label: logged_in_users
osQuery.name: Logged In Users

osQuery.label: disk_encryption_monitoring
osQuery.name: Disk Encryption Status and Details

osQuery.label: operating_system_attributes
osQuery.name: Operating System Attributes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, version, major, minor, patch, build, platform, codename, install_date
FROM os_version;

osQuery.label: listening_ports
osQuery.name: Listening Ports
osQuery.sql:
SELECT p.name, p.path, lp.address, lp.protocol, lp.port 
FROM listening_ports lp LEFT JOIN processes p ON lp.pid = p.pid 
WHERE lp.port != 0 
AND lp.address!="127.0.0.1" 
AND lp.address!="::1" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "c:\windows\system32\%" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "/usr/sbin/%" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "/sbin/%" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "/usr/libexec/%";

osQuery.label: startup_items
osQuery.name: Startup Items
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, path, args, source, type, status, username 
FROM startup_items;

osQuery.label: user_accounts
osQuery.name: User Accounts
osQuery.sql:
SELECT uid, gid, username, description, directory, shell, uuid, type 
FROM users;

osQuery.label: usb_devices
osQuery.name: USB Devices
osQuery.sql:
SELECT usb_address, usb_port, vendor, version, model, serial, class, subclass, protocol, removable 
FROM usb_devices;

osQuery.label: processes_with_network_connections
osQuery.name: Processes With Network Connections

osQuery.label: user_groups_monitoring
osQuery.name: User Groups
osQuery.sql:
SELECT g.groupname, u.username, g.comment
FROM groups g
LEFT JOIN user_groups ug ON g.gid=ug.gid
LEFT JOIN users u ON ug.uid=u.uid;

osQuery.label: mounted_devices_monitoring
osQuery.name: System Mounted Devices and Filesystems
osQuery.sql:
SELECT device, device_alias, path, type, blocks_size, blocks, blocks_free, blocks_available, inodes, inodes_free, flags
FROM mounts;

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_tmp
osQuery.name: Loaded Modules Temp

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_hashes_tmp
osQuery.name: Loaded Modules Hashes Temp

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_hashes
osQuery.name: Loaded Modules Hashes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT module_id, sha256, module_path 
FROM loaded_module_hashes;

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_processes
osQuery.name: Processes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT pid, name AS process_name, path AS process_path 
FROM processes;

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_vs_processes
osQuery.name: Loaded Module ID To Process ID
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
DISTINCT lh.module_id, pmm.pid AS pid 
FROM process_memory_map pmm 
JOIN loaded_module_hashes lh ON lh.module_path=pmm.path;

osQuery.label: wifi_networks_monitoring
osQuery.name: Wi-Fi Networks Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT ssid, network_name, security_type, datetime(last_connected,"unixepoch"), passpoint, possibly_hidden, roaming, roaming_profile, captive_portal, auto_login, temporarily_disabled, disabled
FROM wifi_networks;

osQuery.label: macos_account_policy_data_monitoring
osQuery.name: Account Policy Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
users.username, uid, DATETIME(creation_time, "unixepoch") as "Account Created", failed_login_count,
CASE failed_login_timestamp WHEN "0.0" THEN "0.0" ELSE DATETIME(failed_login_timestamp, "unixepoch") END "Last Failed Login Attempt",
DATETIME(password_last_set_time,  "unixepoch") AS "Password Last Changed"
FROM account_policy_data
JOIN users USING(uid);

osQuery.label: macos_alf_exceptions_monitoring
osQuery.name: Application Layer Firewall Service Exceptions
osQuery.sql:
SELECT path, state 
FROM alf_exceptions;

osQuery.label: macos_alf_explicit_auths_monitoring
osQuery.name: ALF Services Allowed Networking
osQuery.sql:
SELECT process
FROM alf_explicit_auths;

osQuery.label: macos_apps_monitoring
osQuery.name: Installed Applications Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, path, bundle_executable, bundle_identifier, bundle_name, bundle_version, bundle_package_type,
environment, element, development_region, display_name, info_string, category, applescript_enabled, copyright,
datetime(last_opened_time, "unixepoch") AS last_time_used 
FROM apps;

osQuery.label: arp_cache_inspection
osQuery.name: ARPP Cache
osQuery.sql:
SELECT address, mac
FROM arp_cache;

osQuery.label: macos_asl_monitoring
osQuery.name: ASL System Events Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
DATETIME(time, "unixepoch") as time, host, sender, facility, pid, gid, uid,
CASE level
WHEN "0" THEN "Emergency Level"
WHEN "1" THEN "Alert Level"
WHEN "2" THEN "Critical Level"
WHEN "3" THEN "Error Level"
WHEN "4" THEN "Warning Level"
WHEN "5" THEN "Notice Level"
WHEN "6" THEN "Info Level"
WHEN "7" THEN "Debug Level"
ELSE "Unknown" END "Logging Level",
message, extra
FROM asl;

osQuery.label: macos_authorizations_monitoring
osQuery.name: Authorization Rights Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
label, modified,
allow_root, timeout, version, tries, authenticate_user, shared, comment,
created, class, session_owner
FROM authorizations;

osQuery.label: certificates_search
osQuery.name: Certificates
osQuery.sql:
SELECT common_name, subject, issuer, ca, self_signed, not_valid_before, not_valid_after, sha1, path, serial
FROM certificates;

osQuery.label: chrome_extensions_monitoring
osQuery.name: Chrome Extensions

osQuery.label: macos_linux_crontab_monitoring
osQuery.name: System and User Crontab Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT event, minute, hour, day_of_month, month, day_of_week, command, path 
FROM crontab;

osQuery.label: macos_linux_dns_resolvers_monitoring
osQuery.name: DNS Resolvers

osQuery.label: macos_event_taps_monitoring
osQuery.name: Installed Event Taps
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
p1.name AS "Process Being Tapped",
p2.name AS "Tapping Process",
CASE et.enabled WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled " ELSE "Disabled" END "Event Tap Status",
et.event_tap_id,
et.event_tapped
FROM event_taps et
JOIN processes p1 ON p1.pid = et.process_being_tapped
JOIN processes p2 ON p2.pid = et.tapping_process;

osQuery.label: macos_kernel_extensions_monitoring
osQuery.name: Kernel Extensions
osQuery.sql:
SELECT idx, refs, size, name, version, linked_against, path
FROM kernel_extensions;

osQuery.label: macos_keychain_items_monitoring
osQuery.name: Keychain Items Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
label, description, comment, created, modified, type, path
FROM
keychain_items;

osQuery.label: macos_linux_known_hosts_monitoring
osQuery.name: Known Hosts Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
users.username, uid, key, key_file
FROM known_hosts
JOIN users USING(uid);

osQuery.label: macos_linux_last_logins_logouts_monitoring
osQuery.name: Logins/Logouts Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
l.username, l.tty, l.pid, 
p.name AS "process name", 
l.type, 
DATETIME(l.time, "unixepoch") AS "entry timestamp", 
l.host as hostname
FROM last l
LEFT JOIN processes p ON p.pid = l.pid;

osQuery.label: macos_launchd_monitoring
osQuery.name: LaunchAgents and LaunchDaemons Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
path, name, label, program,
run_at_load, keep_alive, disabled,
username, groupname, stdout_path, stderr_path,
start_interval, program_arguments, watch_paths,
queue_directories, inetd_compatibility,
start_on_mount, root_directory,
working_directory, process_type
FROM launchd;

osQuery.label: macos_managed_policies_monitoring
osQuery.name: Managed Policies Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
domain, uuid, name, value, username,
CASE manual WHEN "1" THEN "True" ELSE "False" END "Loaded manually"
FROM managed_policies;

osQuery.label: macos_package_install_history_monitoring
osQuery.name: Package Install History Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
package_id, DATETIME(time, "unixepoch") AS "install_time", name, version, source, content_type
FROM
package_install_history;

osQuery.label: platform_information
osQuery.name: Platform Information
osQuery.sql:
SELECT vendor, version, date, revision, address, size, volume_size, extra
FROM platform_info;

osQuery.label: macos_linux_process_envs_monitoring
osQuery.name: Process Environment Variables Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
p.name AS "Process Name", p.pid, 
pe.key AS "Environment Variable Name", 
pe.value AS "Environment Variable Value"
FROM process_envs pe 
JOIN processes p ON p.pid=pe.pid;

osQuery.label: macos_shared_folders_monitoring
osQuery.name: Shared Folders

osQuery.label: shell_history
osQuery.name: Shell History

osQuery.label: macos_sip_config_monitoring
osQuery.name: System Integrity Protection (SIP) Status
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
config_flag, 
CASE enabled WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled " ELSE "Disabled" END "enabled_status",
CASE enabled_nvram WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled " ELSE "Disabled" END "enabled_nvram_status"
FROM sip_config;

osQuery.label: ssh_configs_monitoring
osQuery.name: SSH Configuration Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT u.username, sc.block, sc.option, sc.ssh_config_file, h.sha256
FROM users u
JOIN ssh_configs sc USING(uid)
JOIN hash h ON h.path = sc.ssh_config_file;

osQuery.label: ssh_user_keys_monitoring
osQuery.name: User SSH Keys Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT u.username, usk.path, usk.encrypted, sha256
FROM users u
JOIN user_ssh_keys usk
USING (uid)
JOIN hash h ON h.path=usk.path;

osQuery.label: macos_linux_sudoers_monitoring
osQuery.name: Sudoers Data

osQuery.label: macos_linux_writable_suid_bin_monitoring
osQuery.name: Writable Suid Binaries Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT f.path, sb.username, sb.groupname, f.mode, sb.permissions
FROM suid_bin sb
LEFT JOIN file f ON sb.path = f.path
WHERE
sb.permissions LIKE regex_match(sb.permissions, "(S|G)", 0) AND
f.mode LIKE regex_match(f.mode, ".*(2|3|6|7)$", 0);

osQuery.label: macos_linux_system_controls
osQuery.name: System Controls Settings
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, oid, subsystem, current_value, config_value, type
FROM system_controls;

osQuery.label: inventory_system_information
osQuery.name: Inventory System Information
osQuery.sql:
SELECT hostname, local_hostname, cpu_type, cpu_subtype, cpu_brand, physical_memory 
FROM system_info;

osQuery.label: time_machine
osQuery.name: Time Machine Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
datetime(b.backup_date,"unixepoch") as "Backup Date", 
destination_id, alias, root_volume_uuid,
bytes_available, bytes_used, encryption 
FROM time_machine_backups AS b
JOIN time_machine_destinations
USING (destination_id);

osQuery.label: macos_xprotect_entries_monitoring
osQuery.name: XProtect Signatures

osQuery.label: macos_xprotect_reports_monitoring
osQuery.name: XProtect Reports Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, user_action, DATETIME(time, "unixepoch") AS "Quarantine Alert" 
FROM xprotect_reports;

queries.title: System Snapshot MacOS 0.0.8 (formerly Forensic Snapshot MacOS 0.0.8)
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. The "UserAssist" registry key contains information about programs that user launched via Windows Explorer. Programs launched via command line do not appear under this key. This query retrieves data logged under the "UserAssist" registry key:
* path - application file path
* most recent time application was executed
* count - number of times the application has been executed
* user SID

osQuery.label: windows_userassist_monitoring
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path,
CASE last_execution_time
WHEN "0" THEN last_execution_time
WHEN "" THEN last_execution_time
ELSE DATETIME(last_execution_time, "unixepoch")
END app_last_execution_time,
count,
sid
FROM userassist
WHERE
path LIKE regex_match(path, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "path_pattern"), 0) AND
app_last_execution_time LIKE regex_match(app_last_execution_time, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "app_last_execution_time_pattern"), 0) 
ORDER BY last_execution_time DESC;

queries.title: UserAssist Registry Data

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It detects a process whose original binary has been deleted or modified. On_disk returns 1 if the process path exists, 0 if it does not, and -1 if it is unknown.

osQuery.label: process_running_without_a_binary_on_disk
osQuery.name: Process Running Without A Binary On Disk
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
pid, name, path, cmdline, state 
FROM processes 
WHERE 
on_disk=0;

queries.title: Process Running Without A Binary On Disk

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. On Windows, a page file on disk is used to hold memory that has been paged out by the virtual memory manager. When investigating an infected host, page file can contain a lot of artifacts that identify malicious behaviour. Malware may attempt to clear the page file at system shutdown to cover its tracks and evade analysis. One way to do this is to set a specific value in the Windows registry. This query monitors the registry value that can delete the page-file at shut-down.

osQuery.label: windows_pagefile_registry_monitoring
osQuery.name: ClearPageFileAtShutdown Registry Key
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management"
AND name LIKE "ClearPageFileAtShutdown"
AND data LIKE "1";

queries.title: Pagefile Configuration Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. The "system32" directory contains important operating system files that Windows needs in order to function properly. Multiple utilities built into Windows such as: 
* The Console Window Host (conhost.exe) 
* The Client Server Runtime System Service (CSRSS) 
* The Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS)
* The Session Manager Subsystem (SMSS)
* The Windows Start-up application (WININIT)
* The Windows Logon Application (WINLOGON)
are located inside this folder. Malware often uses names of legitimate Windows services in order to evade detection. One way to differentiate between legitimate and fake Windows services is to check the location it was launched from. In case of built in Windows services listed above it should always be "%SYSTEMROOT%\system32".

osQuery.label: process_not_launched_from_default_location_system32
osQuery.name: Process Default Location
osQuery.sql:
SELECT p.name, p.path, p.pid, p.parent, p.cmdline 
FROM processes p 
WHERE p.name IN ("conhost.exe", "csrss.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","smss.exe","wininit.exe","winlogon.exe") 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "c:\windows\system32\%" 
AND p.path!="";

queries.title: Process Not Launched From Default Location System32

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Windows Script Host (WSH) is a facility within Windows that executes VBScript and JScript. It is ideal for non-interactive scripting needs, such as logon scripting, administrative scripting, and machine automation. However, it is also often abused by malware for execution and should be disabled on the host if users don't need it.

osQuery.label: windows_registry_wsh_status
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, data, 
CASE data
WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled"
WHEN "0" THEN "Disabled"
END wsh_status,
DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE
path LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows Script Host\Settings\Enabled";

queries.title: Registry Windows Script Host Status

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. This query can detect abuse of Windows' default modules like .NET's csc.exe to compile CSharp source code. This technique is often used for defense evasion and persistence. Following data will be retrieved by this query: 
* datetime - Date and time of the event as stated in eventlogs
* new_process_name - Newly created process name e.g certutil.exe
* new_process_path - Full path of the new process
* parent_process_name - Parent process name only e.g. cmd.exe
* parent_process_path - Full path of the new process
* new_process_commandline - New process commandline
* data - Eventlog data

osQuery.label: persistence_via_hijacking_default_modules
osQuery.name: Default Modules Hijack Attempts
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
LOWER(replace(regex_match(data, 'NewProcessName":"([^"]+)\\([^"]+)', 2), "\\", "\")) AS new_process_name,
replace(regex_match(data, 'NewProcessName":"([^"]+)', 1), "\\", "\") AS new_process_path, 
LOWER(replace(regex_match(data, 'ParentProcessName":"([^"]+)\\([^"]+)', 2), "\\", "\")) AS parent_process_name,
replace(regex_match(data, 'ParentProcessName":"([^"]+)', 1), "\\", "\") AS parent_process_path,
replace(regex_match(data, 'CommandLine":"(.+?),"', 1), "\\", "\") AS new_process_commandline,
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4688 AND
new_process_commandline LIKE regex_match(new_process_commandline, '.*Microsoft\.NET.*Framework64.*csc\.exe\s.*\/out.*\.exe.*\.cs.*',0);

techniques.T1574.008: Path Interception by Search Order Hijacking

queries.title: Persistence via Hijacking Default Modules Monitoring
warnings: This query searches process data from Windows eventlog's audit logs. If the Windows's process event/audit logging is disabled, then this query will not return any data.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves next Windows Event Logs for Netlogon:
* ID "5827" indicating that connection was denied for machine account
* ID "5828" indicating that connection was denied for trust account
* ID "5830" indicating that vulnerable connection was allowed for machine account. This account must be under GPO "Domain controller: Allow vulnerable Netlogon secure channel connection"
* ID "5831" indicating that vulnerable connection was allowed for trust account. This account must be under GPO "Domain controller: Allow vulnerable Netlogon secure channel connection"
* ID "5829" indicating that a vulnerable Netlogon secure channel connection is allowed. This event will not be supported after February 9, 2021 patch.
These events are generated by the Windows patch for August 2020 and its presence on the host does not necessarily indicate malicious activity.
The found events should be used to identify other unpatched systems in the environment or misconfigured GPOs such as "Domain member:Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always)" or "Domain controller: Allow vulnerable Netlogon secure channel connection".
Servers where these logs are not generated should be reviewed for proper patches/mitigations applied or proper set up for Windows Events logging.

osQuery.label: windows_netlogon_events_monitoring
osQuery.name: Netlogon Windows Events Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
data AS Message,
datetime AS "Time Generated",
channel AS LogFile,
provider_name AS SourceName
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "System" AND
eventid IN (5827, 5828, 5830, 5831, 5829);

queries.title: Windows Netlogon Events Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It retrieves process memory data based on the patterns specified. The information retrieved is:
* process name
* path to executed binary
* wired_size - bytes of unpagable memory used by process
* resident_size - bytes of private memory used by process
* total_size - total virtual memory size
* start - virtual start address
* end - virtual end address
* permissions - read (r), write (w), execute (x) or private (cow)

osQuery.label: process_memory_allocation_search
osQuery.name: Process Memory Allocation
osQuery.sql:
SELECT p.name, pmm.path, p.wired_size, p.resident_size, p.total_size, pmm.start, pmm.end, pmm.permissions 
FROM processes p 
LEFT JOIN process_memory_map pmm
ON p.pid=pmm.pid 
WHERE 
p.name LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="process_name") AND 
permissions LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="process_permissions");

queries.title: Process Memory Allocation Search
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. It retrieves Active Directory configuration data from the host:
* name - the OS X-specific configuration name
* domain - active Directory trust domain
* option - canonical name of option
* value - variable typed option value

osQuery.label: macos_ad_config_monitoring
osQuery.name: Active Directory Configuration Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
name, domain, option, value
FROM ad_config
WHERE
domain LIKE regex_match(domain, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "domain_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Active Directory Configuration Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Digest Authentication (WDigest) is a challenge/response protocol for LDAP and web-based authentication. It was introduced in Windows XP and it stores plain text credentials in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS) memory allowing the retrieval through memory scraping or dumping. By default, WDigest is disabled in recent versions of Windows. However, for compatibility reasons, it is still possible to enable this protocol on the host. This query retrieves the list of hosts where WDigest authentication is enabled.

osQuery.label: windows_wdigest_monitoring
osQuery.name: WDigest Registry Key
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\WDigest"
AND name IN ("Negotiate", "UseLogonCredential")
AND data LIKE "1";

queries.title: WDigest Protocol Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves Adobe Reader settings from the registry that could lead to security breaches:
"bEnableJS" - enables JavaScript support. Recommended value is "1" 
"bAllowOpenFile" - allows to open non-PDF attachments in their native application. Recommended value is "0". 
"bSecureOpenFile" - specifies whether to allow opening attachments which are not PDFs. Recommended value is "1".
"bProtectedMode" - enables Protected Mode which sandboxes Acrobat and Reader processes. Recommended value is "1".
"iProtectedView" - specifies whether to never use Protected View (default), only for files from an untrusted location (recommended), or always. Recommended value is "1".
"bEnhancedSecurityInBrowser" - toggles enhanced security when the application is running in the browser. Recommended value is "1".
"bEnhancedSecurityStandalone" - toggles enhanced security for the standalone application. Recommended value is "1".

osQuery.label: windows_registry_acrobat_reader_java_script
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified 
FROM registry
WHERE path IN (
SELECT path || '\JSPrefs\bEnableJS' FROM registry WHERE path LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\SOFTWARE\Adobe\Acrobat Reader\%");

osQuery.label: windows_registry_acrobat_reader_file
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE path IN (
SELECT path || '\Originals\bAllowOpenFile' FROM registry WHERE path LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\SOFTWARE\Adobe\Acrobat Reader\%") OR 
path IN (
SELECT path || '\Originals\bSecureOpenFile' FROM registry WHERE path LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\SOFTWARE\Adobe\Acrobat Reader\%");

osQuery.label: windows_registry_acrobat_reader_protected_mode
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE path IN (
SELECT path || '\Privileged\bProtectedMode' FROM registry WHERE path LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\SOFTWARE\Adobe\Acrobat Reader\%");

osQuery.label: windows_registry_acrobat_reader_protected_view
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE path IN (
SELECT path || '\TrustManager\iProtectedView' FROM registry WHERE path LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\SOFTWARE\Adobe\Acrobat Reader\%");

osQuery.label: windows_registry_acrobat_reader_enhanced_security
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE path IN (
SELECT path || '\TrustManager\bEnhancedSecurityInBrowser' FROM registry WHERE path LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\SOFTWARE\Adobe\Acrobat Reader\%") OR
path IN (
SELECT path || '\TrustManager\bEnhancedSecurityStandalone' FROM registry WHERE path LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\SOFTWARE\Adobe\Acrobat Reader\%");

queries.title: Windows Registry Adobe Acrobat Settings

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Disk encryption is a critical data protection mechanism and is often required as a part of security policy compliance. BitLocker is a Microsoft's proprietary encryption program for Windows. This query retrieves data on BitLocker status from Windows host.

osQuery.label: bitlocker_encryption_monitoring
osQuery.name: Bitlocker Status Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT device_id, drive_letter, persistent_volume_id, conversion_status, protection_status, encryption_method 
FROM bitlocker_info;

queries.title: Bitlocker Status Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It monitors the registry to check if the keys to detect and block new malware using Windows Defender's Cloud Protection have been disabled. Disabling these options may indicate malicious activity.
- DisableBlockAtFirstSeen disables real time checks with Windows Defender Antivirus Cloud Protection before allowing certain content to be run or accessed.
- SpyNetReporting, when set to a value of 0, disables membership to the SpyNet online community that provides information about potential risks or unclassified software.
- SubmitSamplesConsenti, when set to a value of 2, revokes permission to Windows Defender to send samples to the Cloud.

osQuery.label: windows_defender_spynet_registry_monitoring
osQuery.name: Windows Defender Spynet Registry Configuration Monitoring
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") as last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE (key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet"
OR key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet")
AND ((name LIKE "DisableBlockAtFirstSeen"  AND data = "1")
OR (name LIKE "SpyNetReporting" AND data = "0")
OR (name LIKE "SubmitSamplesConsent" AND data = "2"));

queries.title: Windows Defender Spynet Registry Configuration Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. The Network Connections (netman) service manages both local area network and remote connections. Malware may attempt to replace the Service Dynamic Link Library "netman.dll" launched by netman every time it starts. This will lead to malware being able to monitor and control network connections on the host. This query returns data on Service DLL currently used by netman.

osQuery.label: netman_servicedll
osQuery.name: Netman ServiceDLL
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\system\controlset001\services\netman\parameters"
OR key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\system\controlset002\services\netman\parameters";

queries.title: Netman ServiceDLL Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. It retrieves the following data from the MacOS system and application crash logs:
    * uid - user ID of the crashed process
    * username - user name of the crashed process
    * datetime - date/type that the crash occurred
    * pid - pid of the crashed process
    * thread - thread ID which crashed
    * path - path to the crashed process
    * crash_path - location of the log file
    * type - type of crash log
    * identifier - identifier of the crashed process
    * responsible - process responsible for the crashed process
    * exception_type - exception type of the crash
    * exception_codes - exception codes from the crash
    * exception_notes - exception notes from the crash

osQuery.label: macos_crashes_monitoring
osQuery.name: Application, System, and Mobile App Crashes Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
c.uid, u.username,
datetime AS "Date Crash Occured",
c.pid, c.crashed_thread, c.path, c.crash_path, 
c.type, c.identifier, c.responsible, 
c.exception_type, c.exception_codes, c.exception_notes
FROM users u
CROSS JOIN crashes c USING (uid) 
WHERE
u.username LIKE regex_match(u.username, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "username_pattern"), 0) AND
c.path LIKE regex_match(c.path, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "path_pattern"), 0) AND
c.responsible LIKE regex_match(c.responsible, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "responsible_pattern"), 0)
ORDER BY datetime DESC;

queries.title: Application, System, and Mobile App Crashes Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) is used to manage Windows systems both locally and remotely. It is often used by malicious actors for establishing persistence on the host. This query retrives data on WMI filters set on the host:
* name - Unique identifier of an event filter.
* query - Windows Management Instrumentation Query Language (WQL) event query that specifies the set of events for consumer notification, and the specific conditions for notification.
* query_language - Query language that the query is written in.
* class - The name of the class.
* relative_path - Relative path to the class or instance.

osQuery.label: wmi_event_filters
osQuery.name: WMI Event Filters
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, query, query_language, class, relative_path 
FROM wmi_event_filters;

queries.title: WMI Event Filters Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves data on the host registry keys based on the pattern provided:
* registry key name
* registry key value
* registry key value data
* registry path - registry sub-keys 
* date the registry key was last modified

osQuery.label: registry_key_search
osQuery.name: Registry Key
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") as last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="reg_key_name")
AND name LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="reg_key_value")
AND data LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="reg_key_data");

queries.title: Registry Key Search
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. Secure Shell (SSH) is a network protocol that gives users a secure way to access a computer over an insecure network. This query retrieves data on SSH configuration and SSH user keys present on the host: 
* username of the local owner of the ssh_config file 
* SSH block from the configuration file
* SSH option and value from the configuration file
* path to the SSH configuration file
* SHA256 for SSH configuration file
* username of the local user that owns the key file
* path to the SSH user key file
* SSH user key file encryption status
* SHA256 for SSH user key file
Note that this query only retrieves SSH private keys and SSH configuration data for a default SSH location "C:\\Users\\[Username]\\.ssh".

osQuery.label: windows_macos_linux_ssh_configs_monitoring
osQuery.name: SSH Configuration Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT u.username, sc.block, sc.option, sc.ssh_config_file, h.sha256
FROM users u
JOIN ssh_configs sc USING(uid)
JOIN hash h ON h.path = sc.ssh_config_file;

osQuery.label: windows_macos_linux_ssh_private_user_keys_monitoring
osQuery.name: User SSH Private Keys Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
u.username, usk.path, 
CASE usk.encrypted
WHEN "1" THEN "Encrypted"
ELSE "Not Encrypted"
END encryption_status,
sha256
FROM users u
JOIN user_ssh_keys usk
USING (uid)
JOIN hash h ON h.path=usk.path;

queries.title: SSH Configuration Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. A DNS cache is a temporary storage of information about the previous DNS lookups on the host. Monitoring DNS cache records ensures that the DNS continues to route websites and services traffic properly and could help detect such attacks as DNS cache poisoning. This query retrieves DNS cache data:
* domain name 
* type - DNS record type

osQuery.label: dns_cache_table_monitoring
osQuery.name: DNS Cache Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name AS "domain", type
FROM dns_cache
WHERE 
name LIKE regex_match(name, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "domain_pattern"), 0) AND 
type LIKE regex_match(type, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "type_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: DNS Cache Monitoring
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It retrieves name of a network interface along with it's IP address of the host. It ignores loop-back interfaces:
* interface - interface name
* address - interface address
* mask - interface netmask
* type - type of address: dhcp, manual, auto, other, or unknown
* friendly_name  - the friendly display name of the interface

osQuery.label: interface_names_and_associated_ips
osQuery.name: Interface Names And Associated IPs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
interface, address, mask, type, friendly_name 
FROM interface_addresses 
WHERE 
interface NOT LIKE "lo%" AND 
interface!=1;

queries.title: Interface Names And Associated IPs
warnings: This query excludes loopback interfaces.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Linux and MacOS. It parses the lines of the system and user cron/tab files and returns the contents:
    * event - The job @event name (rare).
    * minute - The exact minute the job is scheduled.
    * hour - The hour of the day the job is scheduled.
    * day_of_month - The day of the month the job is scheduled.
    * month - The month of the year the job is scheduled. 
    * day_of_week - The day of the week the job is scheduled.
    * command - The raw command string for the scheduled job.
    * path - The file where the job line is located (file parsed\.

osQuery.label: macos_linux_crontab_monitoring
osQuery.name: System and User Crontab Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
event, minute, hour, day_of_month, month, day_of_week, command, path 
FROM crontab
WHERE
command LIKE regex_match(command, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "command_pattern"), 0) AND
path LIKE regex_match(path, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "path_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Crontab Monitoring
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves information for system physical disk drives:
* partitions - Number of detected partitions on disk.
* disk_index - Physical drive number of the disk.
* type - The interface type of the disk.
* id - The unique identifier of the drive on the system.
* pnp_device_id - Windows Plug and Play device identifier of the logical device.
* disk_size - Size of the disk in bytes.
* manufacturer - The manufacturer of the disk.
* hardware_model - Hard drive model.
* name - The label of the disk object.
* serial - The serial number of the disk.
* description - The OS's description of the disk.

osQuery.label: windows_disk_info_monitoring
osQuery.name: System Disk Information
osQuery.sql:
SELECT partitions, disk_index, type, id, pnp_device_id, disk_size, manufacturer, hardware_model, name, serial, description
FROM disk_info
WHERE partitions LIKE regex_match(partitions, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "partitions_number"), 0) AND
type LIKE regex_match(type, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "type_pattern"), 0) AND
pnp_device_id LIKE regex_match(pnp_device_id, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "pnp_device_id_pattern"), 0) AND
disk_size LIKE regex_match(disk_size, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "disk_size_pattern"), 0) AND
hardware_model LIKE regex_match(hardware_model, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "hardware_model_pattern"), 0) AND
serial LIKE regex_match(serial, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "serial_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Windows Disk Info Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to MacOS. The OpenSSL versions 3.0.0-3.0.7 and 1.0.2-1.1.1 are vulnerable to multiple critical vulnerabilities.

osQuery.label: macos_openssl_vulnerable_versions_three_series
osQuery.name: OpenSSL Vulnerable Versions in HomeBrew Packages
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name,
version
FROM homebrew_packages
WHERE name LIKE '%openssl%' AND
(((CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 307) AND
(CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) > 300)) OR
((CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 111) AND
(CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) > 101))) ;

osQuery.label: macos_openssl_vulnerable_versions_one_series
osQuery.name: OpenSSL Vulnerable Versions in Binaries
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
(path LIKE "/opt/local/bin/%" OR path LIKE "/usr/local/bin/%") AND
sigrule='rule openssl_vulnerable_versions { meta: author = "Talos" strings: $s1 = /OpenSSL(\s)?3\.[0-9]{1}.[0-6]{1}/ nocase ascii $s2 = /OpenSSL(\s)?[(0-1)]{1}\.[0-9]{1}.[(0|8|2)]{1}/ nocase ascii condition: any of them}' AND  
matches!="";

queries.title: MacOS OpenSSL Vulnerable Versions Monitoring
warnings: This query runs yara scan on some common paths to mitigate resource usage. You can run it manually and change the paths according to your environment.

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It generates process and port information based on a specific port number passed as the parameter. The result includes the process id, process name, full path of the process, IP address, protocol, and port number.

osQuery.label: search_processes_by_listening_port
osQuery.name: Search Processes by Listening Port

queries.title: Search Processes by Listening Port

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves the set of executables that automatically execute on the host. This includes services, scheduled_tasks, and startup_items. The following information is retrieved: | 
* path - Path to the executable 
* name - Name of the program 
* source - Source of the autoexec item such as scheduled_tasks or startup_items
* sha256

osQuery.label: windows_executables_that_automatically_execute
osQuery.name: Windows executables that automatically execute
osQuery.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT ae.name, ae.path, ae.source, h.sha256 
FROM autoexec ae LEFT JOIN hash h ON h.path = ae.path;

queries.title: Windows executables that automatically execute
warnings: Due to SHA256 hashing, this query can take some time in returning results.

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Windows password filters are Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs) used to validate passwords against password policies. Malware could register a malicious password filter to harvest user credentials.

osQuery.label: windows_password_filters_monitoring
osQuery.name: Password Filters Registry
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\system\currentcontrolset\control\lsa"
AND name LIKE "Notification Packages";

queries.title: Windows Password Filters Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It is designed to retrieve the last 500 PowerShell script blocks allowing a review of PowerShell command history on the host. Note that PowerShell ScriptBlock logging must be enabled on the endpoint for this query to function.

osQuery.label: powershell_history
osQuery.name: PowerShell History
osQuery.sql:
SELECT time, datetime, script_block_id, script_block_count, script_text, script_name, script_path
FROM orbital_powershell_events
ORDER BY datetime DESC
LIMIT 500;

queries.title: PowerShell History Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves following data on system logon sessions:
* username
* the name of the domain used to authenticate the owner of the logon session
* the authentication package used to authenticate the owner of the logon session
* the logon method - interactive, service, network, remote interactive, batch, new credentials, undefined logon type
* the logon time
* the name of the server used to authenticate the owner of the logon session
* the DNS name for the owner of the logon session
* the user principal name (UPN) for the owner of the logon session
* the script used for logging on
* the home directory for the logon session
* the drive location of the home directory of the logon session

osQuery.label: logon_sessions_monitoring
osQuery.name: Logon Sessions

queries.title: Logon Sessions Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves printer configurations on the system. Data displayed are:
* Printer name
* Direct - if True, the print job is sent directly to the printer. If False, the print job is spooled.
* DoCompleteFirst - if TRUE, the printer starts jobs that are finished spooling. If FALSE, the printer starts jobs in the order that the jobs are received.
* KeepPrintedJobs - if TRUE, the print spooler does not delete the completed jobs.
* Local - if TRUE, the printer is not attached to a network. If both the Local and Network properties are set to TRUE, then the printer is a network printer.
* Network - if TRUE, the printer is a network printer.
* PortName - port that is used to transmit data to a printer.
* PrintJobDataType - data type of a print job waiting for the Windows-based printing device.
* PrintProcessor - name of the print spooler that handles print jobs.
* Priority - priority of the printer. Jobs on a higher priority printer are scheduled first.
* Queued - if TRUE, the printer buffers and queues print jobs.
* RawOnly - if TRUE, the printer accepts only raw data to be spooled.
* Shared - if TRUE, the printer is available as a shared network resource.
* WorkOffline - if TRUE, you can queue print jobs on the computer when the printer is offline.
* PrinterPaperNames - paper sizes supported by the printer.
* CapabilityDescriptions - the printer features
This query could be used to solve such tasks as to show which printers can print in color, show status of printer and print jobs, identify default printer on the system.
** 1 means True, 0 means False

osQuery.label: windows_printers_monitoring
osQuery.name: Printers Configuration
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
Name,
Direct,
DoCompleteFirst,
KeepPrintedJobs,
Local,
Network,
PortName,
PrintJobDataType,
PrintProcessor,
Priority,
Queued,
RawOnly,
Shared,
WorkOffline,
PrinterPaperNames,
CapabilityDescriptions
FROM Win32_Printer;

queries.title: Printers Configuration Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Linux. It monitors the history of shell commands executed on the host: 
* username 
* time - entry timestamp, could be absent, default value is 0
* command - unparsed date/line/command history line
* history_file - path to the .*_history for the user

osQuery.label: shell_history
osQuery.name: Shell History
osQuery.sql:
SELECT u.username, sh.time, sh.command, sh.history_file 
FROM users u
CROSS JOIN shell_history sh USING (uid)
WHERE
sh.command LIKE regex_match(sh.command, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "command_line_pattern"), 0) AND
u.username LIKE regex_match(u.username, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "username_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Shell History Monitoring
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It searches the Windows Event Logs for events with Event ID "4625" and "Sub Status" code of "0xC0000193" which represents failed logon events where the account trying to login is expired. This could indicate malicious behaviour or that a formerly authorized account is trying to gain access to the system. All hosts where such Windows Logs are detected should be investigated.

osQuery.label: windows_events_expired_account_usage_monitoring
osQuery.name: Expired Account Failed Logins Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
regex_match(data, '"TargetUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS DisabledUserName,
data AS Message,
datetime AS "Time Generated",
channel AS LogFile,
provider_name AS SourceName
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4625 AND
data LIKE '%"SubStatus":"0xc0000193"%';

queries.title: Expired Account Usage Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It will detect whether the target is vulnerable to Microsoft Office Outlook privilege escalation vulnerability (CVE-2023-23397) that allows for an NTLM Relay attack against another service to authenticate as the user. This query will display name, version and vulnerablilty status of following vulnerable MS Office and Outlook versions:
- Volume licensed versions of Office 2019 - prior to build  10396.20023
- Retail versions of Office 2016 C2R and Office 2019 - prior to build 16130.20332
- MS Office 2016 - prior to build 16.0.5537.1000
- MS Outlook 2013 Service Pack 1 - prior to 15.0.5537.1000

osQuery.label: cve_2023_23397_monitoring
osQuery.name: MS Office Products' Vulnerability Status
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, version,
CASE
WHEN version LIKE "%15.%" AND version < "15.0.5537.1000" THEN "Vulnerable"
WHEN version LIKE "%16.%" AND version < "16.0.5387.1000" THEN "Vulnerable"
WHEN version LIKE "%14.%" THEN "Vulnerable"
WHEN name LIKE "%Microsoft Outlook %" THEN "Vulnerable"
WHEN version LIKE regex_match(version,"\d{5}\.\d{5}", 0) AND (version < "16130.20306" OR version < "10396.20023") THEN "Vulnerable"
ELSE "Not Vulnerable"
END "CVE-2023-23397 Status" FROM programs
WHERE (name LIKE "%Microsoft Office %" or name LIKE "% Outlook %") ;

queries.title: CVE-2023-23397 Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves the types of information that will be gathered from memory when the operating system fails. This includes boot failures and system crashes. Data collected are:
* AutoReboot - if True, system will automatically reboot during a recovery operation
* DebugFilePath - full path to the debug file
* DebugInfoType - type of debugging information written to the log file
* OverwriteExistingDebugFile - if True, new debug file will overwrite an existing one
* SendAdminAlert - if True, alert message will be sent to the system administrator in the event of an operating system failure
* WriteDebugInfo - if True, debugging information is to be written to a log file
* WriteToSystemLog - if True, events will be written to a system log
** 1 means True, 0 means False

osQuery.label: os_recovery_configuration_monitoring
osQuery.name: OS Recovery Configuration
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
AutoReboot,
DebugFilePath,
CASE DebugInfoType
WHEN "0" THEN "None"
WHEN "1" THEN "Complete memory dump"
WHEN "2" THEN "Kernel memory dump"
WHEN "3" THEN "Small memory dump"
WHEN "7" THEN "Automatic memory dump"
END "DebugInfoType",
OverwriteExistingDebugFile,
SendAdminAlert,
WriteDebugInfo,
WriteToSystemLog
FROM Win32_OSRecoveryConfiguration;

queries.title: OS Recovery Configuration Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Most hardware peripherals use device drivers that have been digitally signed by Microsoft. This means that the driver is associated with a digital certificate allowing identification of the publisher of the driver. Windows allows signed drivers to be automatically installed and ensures its integrity. However, a user can allow installation of unsigned drivers on the system. Presence of an unsigned driver is not necessarily malicious, but could represent potential security breach. This query retrieves all unsigned drivers on the host.

osQuery.label: windows_unsigned_drivers
osQuery.name: Windows Unsigned Drivers

queries.title: Windows Unsigned Drivers Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Dynamic Link Library (DLL) injection is used to run code within the existing process by forcing this process to load a specific DLL. Malware can use DLL injection to execute malicious code within the context of a benign process. This query retrieves the list of suspicious DLLs that are loaded by system processes on the host.

osQuery.label: monitoring_dlls_used_by_running_processes
osQuery.name: Running Processes DLLs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT p.name as process_name, pm.path as dll_name 
FROM processes p 
JOIN process_memory_map pm 
ON p.pid=pm.pid 
WHERE p.path LIKE "%:\windows%" 
AND pm.path != ""
AND pm.path NOT LIKE "%windows\system32%"
AND pm.path LIKE "%.dll";

queries.title: Monitoring DLLs Used By Running Processes
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) is used to manage Windows systems both locally and remotely. It is often used by malicious actors for establishing persistence on the host. This query retrives data on WMI consumers with type "CommandLineEventConsumer" designed to run arbitrary process for specific events: 
* name - Unique name of a consumer.
* command_line_template - Standard string template that specifies the process to be started. This property can be NULL, and the ExecutablePath property is used as the command line.
* executable_path - Module to execute. The string can specify the full path and file name of the module to execute, or it can specify a partial name. If a partial name is specified, the current drive and current directory are assumed.
* class - The name of the class.
* relative_path - Relative path to the class or instance.

osQuery.label: wmi_command_line_event_consumer
osQuery.name: WMI Command Line Event Consumer
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, command_line_template, executable_path, class, relative_path
FROM wmi_cli_event_consumers;

queries.title: WMI Command Line Event Consumer Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. On a Windows system, it is possible to enable accessibility features on the login screen. Those features run an executable as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. These executables can be replaced with others such as cmd.exe, which will let an attacker run a privileged shell on the target host without being authenticated.

osQuery.label: sticky_keys_registry_backdoor
osQuery.name: Sticky Keys Registry
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key as reg_key, path, name, data, datetime(mtime,"unixepoch") AS last_modified 
FROM registry 
WHERE name LIKE "Debugger"
AND 
(key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\utilman.exe" OR
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\osk.exe" OR
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\magnify.exe" OR
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\narrator.exe" OR
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\displayswitch.exe" OR
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\atbroker.exe" OR 
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\sethc.exe" OR 
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\helppane.exe");

queries.title: Sticky Keys Registry Backdoor

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. This query will retrieve the commands which tried to change the executable paths (binpath) of service to powershell.exe from Windows eventlog.

osQuery.label: service_binpath_being_set_to_powershell
osQuery.name: Modified Service Binpaths
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
datetime,
LOWER(replace(regex_match(data, 'NewProcessName":"([^"]+)\\([^"]+)', 2), "\\", "\")) AS new_process_name,
replace(regex_match(data, 'NewProcessName":"([^"]+)', 1), "\\", "\") AS new_process_path, 
LOWER(replace(regex_match(data, 'ParentProcessName":"([^"]+)\\([^"]+)', 2), "\\", "\")) AS parent_process_name,
replace(regex_match(data, 'ParentProcessName":"([^"]+)', 1), "\\", "\") AS parent_process_path,
replace(regex_match(data, 'CommandLine":"(.+?)",', 1), "\\", "\") AS new_process_commandline,
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4688 AND 
new_process_commandline LIKE regex_match(LOWER(new_process_commandline), '.*sc\s+config\s+.*binpath=.*powershell\.exe.*', 0);

queries.title: Modify Service To Run PowerShell Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves Windows Event Log with Event ID "4706" generated when a new trust is created to a Domain Controller, Windows Event Log with Event ID "4707" indicating the removal of a trusted domain relationship from the primary domain controller, and Windows Event Log with Event ID "4716" generated when the modification of the trusted domain information was performed. Systems where these logs are present should be investigated since this could be related to malicious actors attempting to establish persistence or evade detection on a compromised host.

osQuery.label: windows_events_domain_information_modification_monitoring
osQuery.name: Domain Information Modification Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
data AS Message,
datetime AS "Time Generated",
channel AS LogFile,
provider_name AS SourceName
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid IN (4706, 4707, 4716);

queries.title: Domain Information Modification Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves information for in-use Windows device drivers. This data do not include installed but unused drivers:
* device_id - device ID
* device_name - device name
* image - path to driver image file
* description - driver description
* service - driver service name, if one exists
* service_key - driver service registry key
* version - driver version
* inf - associated inf file
* class - device/driver class name
* provider - driver provider
* manufacturer - device manufacturer
* driver_key - driver key
* date - driver date
* signed - whether the driver is signed or not

osQuery.label: windows_device_drivers_monitoring
osQuery.name: Windows Device Drivers
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
device_id, device_name, image, description, service, service_key, version, inf, class, provider, manufacturer, driver_key, 
DATETIME(date, "unixepoch") AS "Driver_date", signed
FROM drivers
WHERE
device_id LIKE regex_match(device_id, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "device_id_pattern"), 0) AND
device_name LIKE regex_match(device_name, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "device_name_pattern"), 0) AND
service LIKE regex_match(service, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "service_pattern"), 0) AND
inf LIKE regex_match(inf, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "inf_pattern"), 0) AND
signed LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "signed_value");

queries.title: Windows Device Drivers Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. Speculative execution is an optimization technique to improve concurrency in CPUs. The processor executes code subsequent to a branch before knowing whether the branch will in-fact be taken or not. Vulnerabilities in speculative execution implementation, such as Meltdown and Spectre, allow a rogue process to read all memory without proper authorization exploiting a race condition. This query retrieves data related to speculative execution features enabled on the host.

The following settings are recommended:
* bp_mitigations should always return 1, as it indicates Branch Prediction mitigations are enabled.
* bp_system_pol_disabled should always be set to 0
* bp_microcode_disabled should always be set to 0

osQuery.label: kva_information_search
osQuery.name: KVA Information
osQuery.sql:
SELECT kva_shadow_enabled, kva_shadow_user_global, kva_shadow_pcid, kva_shadow_inv_pcid, bp_mitigations, bp_system_pol_disabled, bp_microcode_disabled, cpu_spec_ctrl_supported, ibrs_support_enabled, stibp_support_enabled, cpu_pred_cmd_supported
FROM kva_speculative_info;

queries.title: KVA Information Search

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows, Linux, and MacOS (Intel only). It retrieves data on CPU features set on the host.

osQuery.label: cpu_feature_search
osQuery.name: CPU Features

queries.title: CPU Feature Search
warnings: This query may produce excessive amounts of CPU data. Changing parameters to a specific set of relevant features is advised.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. It returns the current WiFi status:
    * interface - the name of the WiFi interface
    * ssid - network Service Set Identifier (SSID)
    * bssid - the current basic SSID
    * network_name - the name of the network
    * country_code - the country code (ISO/IEC 3166-1:1997) for the network
    * security_type - the type of security on this network
    * mode - the current operating mode for the WiFi interface

osQuery.label: macos_wifi_status_monitoring
osQuery.name: WiFi Status Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
interface, ssid, bssid, network_name,
country_code, security_type, mode 
FROM wifi_status
WHERE 
network_name LIKE regex_match(network_name, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "network_name_pattern"), 0) AND
security_type LIKE regex_match(security_type, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "security_type_patten"), 0);

queries.title: WiFi Status Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Linux. It helps to speed up the Log4j vulnerability investigation and remediation cycle Log4j by collecting next data:
* identify log4j DEB package installed on the host
* identify Java processes with environmental variable "LOG4J_FORMAT_MSG_NO_LOOKUPS" not present. If this variable is not set - the process could be log4j vulnerable
* search process memory mapped files for loaded log4j or jar files
* get current files opened by java processes and search for log4j package
* scan server logs with YARA rules designed by Florian Roth for potential compromise events

osQuery.label: log4j_deb_packages
osQuery.name: DEB Packages
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
name, version, source, revision, status, maintainer
FROM
deb_packages
WHERE
name LIKE "%log4j%";

osQuery.label: log4j_format_msg_no_lookups_envar
osQuery.name: LOG4J_FORMAT_MSG_NO_LOOKUPS Envar
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
p.name, pe.key, pe.value
FROM processes p
JOIN process_envs pe USING(pid)
WHERE
pe.key LIKE "%LOG4J_FORMAT_MSG_NO_LOOKUPS%";

osQuery.label: process_memory_map
osQuery.name: Loaded Modules
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
DISTINCT p.name, pmm.path
FROM process_memory_map pmm
JOIN processes p USING(pid)
WHERE
pmm.path LIKE "%jar%" OR pmm.path LIKE "%log4j%";

osQuery.label: current_open_files
osQuery.name: Process Open Files
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
p.name, pof.fd, pof.path
FROM process_open_files pof
JOIN processes p USING(pid)
WHERE
pof.path LIKE "%log4j%";

osQuery.label: yara_EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_JAVA_Exception_Dec21_1
osQuery.name: EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_JAVA_Exception_Dec21_1
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
path LIKE "/var/log/%%" AND
sigrule='rule EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_JAVA_Exception_Dec21_1 { meta: author = "Florian Roth" strings: $xa1 = "header with value of BadAttributeValueException: " $sa1 = ".log4j.core.net.JndiManager.lookup(JndiManager" $sa2 = "Error looking up JNDI resource" condition: $xa1 or all of ($sa*) }' AND
matches!="";

osQuery.label: yara_EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_Soft
osQuery.name: EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_Soft
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
path LIKE "/var/log/%%" AND 
sigrule='rule EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_Soft { meta: author = "Florian Roth" strings: $x01 = "${jndi:ldap:/" $x02 = "${jndi:rmi:/" $x03 = "${jndi:ldaps:/" $x04 = "${jndi:dns:/" $x05 = "${jndi:iiop:/" $x06 = "${jndi:http:/" $x07 = "${jndi:nis:/" $x08 = "${jndi:nds:/" $x09 = "${jndi:corba:/" $fp1 = "<html" condition: 1 of ($x*) and not 1 of ($fp*)}' AND
matches!="";

osQuery.label: yara_EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_OBFUSC
osQuery.name: EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_OBFUSC
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
path LIKE "/var/log/%%" AND
sigrule='rule EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_OBFUSC { meta: author = "Florian Roth" strings: $x1 = "$%7Bjndi:" $x2 = "%2524%257Bjndi" $x3 = "%2F%252524%25257Bjndi%3A" $x4 = "${jndi:${lower:" $x5 = "${::-j}${" $x6 = "${${env:BARFOO:-j}" $x7 = "${::-l}${::-d}${::-a}${::-p}" $x8 = "${base64:JHtqbmRp" $fp1 = "<html" condition: 1 of ($x*) and not 1 of ($fp*) }' AND
matches!="";

osQuery.label: yara_EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_Hard
osQuery.name: EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_Hard
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
path LIKE "/var/log/%%" AND
sigrule='rule EXPL_Log4j_CVE_2021_44228_Dec21_Hard { meta: author = "Florian Roth" strings: $x1 = /\$\{jndi:(ldap|ldaps|rmi|dns|iiop|http|nis|nds|corba):\/[\/]?[a-z-\.0-9]{3,120}:[0-9]{2,5}\/[a-zA-Z\.]{1,32}\}/ $x2 = "Reference Class Name: foo" $fp1r = /(ldap|rmi|ldaps|dns):\/[\/]?(127\.0\.0\.1|192\.168\.|172\.[1-3][0-9]\.|10\.)/ condition: 1 of ($x*) and not 1 of ($fp*) }' AND
matches!="";

osQuery.label: yara_SUSP_JDNIExploit_Error_Indicators_Dec21_1
osQuery.name: SUSP_JDNIExploit_Error_Indicators_Dec21_1
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
path LIKE "/var/log/%%" AND
sigrule='rule SUSP_JDNIExploit_Error_Indicators_Dec21_1 { meta: author = "Florian Roth" strings: $x1 = "FATAL log4j - Message: BadAttributeValueException: " condition: $x1 }' AND
matches!="";

osQuery.label: yara_SUSP_JDNIExploit_Indicators_Dec21
osQuery.name: SUSP_JDNIExploit_Indicators_Dec21
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
path LIKE "/var/log/%%" AND
sigrule='rule SUSP_JDNIExploit_Indicators_Dec21 { meta: author = "Florian Roth" strings: $xr1 = /(ldap|ldaps|rmi|dns|iiop|http|nis|nds|corba):\/\/[a-zA-Z0-9\.]{7,80}:[0-9]{2,5}\/(Basic\/Command\/Base64|Basic\/ReverseShell|Basic\/TomcatMemshell|Basic\/JBossMemshell|Basic\/WebsphereMemshell|Basic\/SpringMemshell|Basic\/Command|Deserialization\/CommonsCollectionsK|Deserialization\/CommonsBeanutils|Deserialization\/Jre8u20\/TomcatMemshell|Deserialization\/CVE_2020_2555\/WeblogicMemshell|TomcatBypass|GroovyBypass|WebsphereBypass)\// condition: filesize < 100MB and $xr1 }' AND
matches!="";

queries.title: Linux Log4j Monitoring
warnings: This query can take some time to load the results due to YARA scans.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It retrieved the following data on the host basic active kernel information:
* kernel version 
* kernel arguments 
* kernel path
* kernel device identifier 
* sha256

osQuery.label: kernel_information
osQuery.name: Kernel Information
osQuery.sql:
SELECT ki.version, ki.arguments, ki.path, ki.device, hash.sha256 
FROM kernel_info ki JOIN hash ON ki.path=hash.path;

queries.title: Kernel Information Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It retrieves data on processes currently running on the host:
* pid - process ID
* name - process name 
* path - path to executed binary
* cmdline - process command line 
* state - process state: exited, still active
* SHA256 of the executed binary

osQuery.label: sha256_hash_of_running_processes
osQuery.name: SHA256 Hash Of Running Processes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
p.pid, p.name, p.path, p.cmdline, p.state, h.sha256 
FROM processes p 
INNER JOIN hash h 
ON p.path=h.path;

queries.title: SHA256 Hash Of Running Processes
warnings: This query may be slow and produce large volume of results if the host has a high number of processes
Query results are limited to processes that have a file on disk

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves Windows events generated when a new computer account was created on the host with "User Account Attribute: Password Not Required" flag set to "Enabled" which could indicate an attacker trying to gain persistence on the host: 
* datetime the event was generated
* target_user_name - the name of the account that requested the "create user account" operation
* target_domain_name - subject's domain or computer name
* subject_user_name - the name of the user account that was created
* subject_domain_name - domain name of created user account
* user_account_control - user account control attribute of new user object: password, lockout, disable/enable, script and etc. 
* data - full event data

osQuery.label: windows_eventlogs_computer_account_created
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
regex_match(data, 'TargetUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS target_user_name, 
regex_match(data, 'TargetDomainName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS target_domain_name,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_user_name,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectDomainName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_domain_name,
regex_match(data, 'UserAccountControl":"([^"]+)', 1) AS user_account_control,
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid IN (4720, 4741)  AND
subject_user_name LIKE "%$" AND
user_account_control LIKE "%2082%";

queries.title: Windows Event Logs: Computer Account Created

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves hosts where a user enabled DNS-over-HTTPS. This can be used to hide internet activity or the process of exfiltrating data.

osQuery.label: windows_registry_dns_over_https
osQuery.name: Registry
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data,
DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE (
path LIKE 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\BuiltInDnsClientEnabled' OR
