path LIKE 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Google\Chrome\DnsOverHttpsMode' OR
path LIKE 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Mozilla\Firefox\DNSOverHTTPS\%');

queries.title: Windows Registry DNS Over HTTPS Enabled

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It can retrieve Windows run registry keys' entries which may contain malicious entries. These entries are executed on system startup, hence attackers can abuse them to achieve persistence on the target hosts.

osQuery.label: autorun_registry_keys_monitoring
osQuery.name: Registry Run Keys
osQuery.sql:
SELECT datetime(mtime,"unixepoch") AS last_modified, key as reg_key, name, data
FROM registry 
WHERE 
(key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run%" OR
key LIKE  "HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run%" OR
key LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run%" OR
key LIKE "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run%")
OR  (key LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Microsoft\Command Processor"
AND name LIKE "AutoRun");

queries.title: Windows Run Registry Keys Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. The Print Processors are user-mode Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs) that are responsible for converting a print job's spooled data into a format that can be sent to a print monitor. They are also responsible for handling application requests to pause, resume and cancel print jobs. Malware, such as Winnti, registers malicious DLL loaders as alternative Print Processors by modifying "Print Processors" registry keys to establish persistence. This query retrieves data on all registered Print Processors DLL on the host.

osQuery.label: registered_print_processors
osQuery.name: Print Processors DLL Registry Keys
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, name,  data, datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified 
FROM registry 
WHERE (key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CONTROLSET001\CONTROL\PRINT\ENVIRONMENTS\WINDOWS X64\PRINT PROCESSORS\%"
OR key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CONTROLSET002\CONTROL\PRINT\ENVIRONMENTS\WINDOWS X64\PRINT PROCESSORS\%"
OR key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CURRENTCONTROLSET\CONTROL\PRINT\ENVIRONMENTS\WINDOWS X64\PRINT PROCESSORS\%")
AND name LIKE "Driver";

osQuery.label: print_processors_file_data
osQuery.name: Print Processors DLL Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT f.path, f.directory, f.filename, h.sha256,
datetime(f.atime, "unixepoch") as last_accessed,
datetime(f.mtime, "unixepoch") as last_modified,
datetime(f.btime, "unixepoch") as created
FROM file f JOIN hash h ON h.path = f.path 
WHERE f.path LIKE (SELECT value FROM orbital_environment WHERE name LIKE "windir") || "\System32\spool\prtprocs\x64\%.dll";

queries.title: Print Processor Dynamic Link Libraries Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It returns data on system shared resources such as disk drives, printers, interprocess communications, or other shareable devices. The following data is retrieved:
* description 
* install_date - lack of value does not indicate that the object is not installed 
* status 
* allow_maximum - number of concurrent users for this device has been limited
* maximum_allowed - limit on the maximum number of users allowed to use this resource concurrently
* name 
* path 
* type

osQuery.label: shared_resources_monitoring
osQuery.name: Shared Resources
osQuery.sql:
SELECT description, install_date, status, allow_maximum, maximum_allowed, name, path, type
FROM shared_resources;

queries.title: Shared Resources Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. The InprocServer32 key under the CLSID points to the class's DLL location and the threading model. This value can be overwritten by the attckers to the malicous DLL. This query will show such keys with DLL locations present outside the trusted folders. Following data will be retrieved by this query:
reg_path - Full path of the registry 
name - Registry name, which will be (Default) 
type - Type of the data
data - Location of the DLL

Note: The results will be available in "InprocServer32 Default Suspicious Entries" tab. The other two tabs' data can be ignored because they are a step to fetch the final results.

osQuery.label: user_clsids_tmp
osQuery.name: HKEY User CLSIDs
osQuery.sql:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE user_clsids AS  
SELECT SPLIT(path,"\",5) AS clsid, SPLIT(path, "\", 1) AS user_sid FROM registry WHERE  key LIKE 'HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Classes\CLSID';

osQuery.label: inproc_table_tmp
osQuery.name: InprocServer32 Keys
osQuery.sql:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE inproc_table AS
SELECT 
"HKEY_USERS\" || user_sid || "\Software\Classes\CLSID\" || clsid || "\InProcServer32" AS inproc_key 
FROM user_clsids;

osQuery.label: com_hijack_inprocserver32_key_monitoring
osQuery.name: InprocServer32 Default Suspicious Entries
osQuery.sql:
SELECT path as reg_path, name, type, data
FROM registry
WHERE
(key IN (SELECT inproc_key FROM inproc_table)
OR key LIKE 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Classes\CLSID\%\InprocServer32') 
AND name='(Default)'
AND data NOT LIKE "%\System32\%"
AND data NOT LIKE "%\Program Files%"
AND data NOT LIKE "%CommonProgramFiles%"
AND data NOT LIKE "%ProgramFiles%"
AND data NOT LIKE "%SystemRoot%"
AND data LIKE "%\%";

queries.title: COM Hijack By Modifying InprocServer32 Registry Key Monitoring

starting540.label: user_clsids_tmp
starting540.name: HKEY User CLSIDs
starting540.sql:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE user_clsids AS  
SELECT SPLIT(path,"\",5) AS clsid, SPLIT(path, "\", 1) AS user_sid FROM registry WHERE  key LIKE 'HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Classes\CLSID';

starting540.label: inproc_table_tmp
starting540.name: InprocServer32 Keys
starting540.sql:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE inproc_table AS
SELECT 
"HKEY_USERS\" || user_sid || "\Software\Classes\CLSID\" || clsid || "\InProcServer32" AS inproc_key 
FROM user_clsids;

starting540.label: com_hijack_inprocserver32_key_monitoring
starting540.name: InprocServer32 Default Suspicious Entries
starting540.sql:
SELECT path as reg_path, name, type, data from registry 
WHERE 
(key LIKE 'HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Classes\CLSID\%\InprocServer32' 
OR key LIKE 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Classes\CLSID\%\InprocServer32')
AND name='(Default)' 
AND data NOT LIKE "%\System32\%" 
AND data NOT LIKE "%\Program Files%" 
AND data NOT LIKE "%CommonProgramFiles%"
AND data NOT LIKE "%ProgramFiles%" 
AND data NOT LIKE "%SystemRoot%" 
AND data LIKE "%\%";

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Linux and MacOS. It is designed to find setuid binaries that are modifiable by users other than their owner. The use of setuid allows for users to execute a binary and get the necessary privileges to execute it correctly. This is a preferable method to gaining some additional privileges over adding more users to the sudoers file. This can be used maliciously to execute code in a different user's context. Data displayed:
* path - binary path
* username - binary owner username
* groupname - binary owner group
* mode - permission bits
* permissions - binary permissions

osQuery.label: macos_linux_writable_suid_bin_monitoring
osQuery.name: Writable Suid Binaries Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT f.path, sb.username, sb.groupname, f.mode, sb.permissions
FROM suid_bin sb
LEFT JOIN file f ON sb.path = f.path
WHERE
sb.permissions LIKE regex_match(sb.permissions, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "permissions_pattern"), 0) AND
f.mode LIKE regex_match(f.mode, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "mode_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Writable Suid Binaries Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Windows Authentication Packages DLLs are loaded by the Local Security Authority (LSA) service at system start providing support for logon processes and security protocols. Malware often modifies registry settings for Authentication Packages DLLs by adding a malicious DLL to it or by replacing the original legitimate DLL with a malicious DLL in order to gain persistence on the host.

osQuery.label: windows_authentication_package_monitoring
osQuery.name: Authentication Packages
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\system\currentcontrolset\control\lsa"
AND name="Authentication Packages";

queries.title: Windows Authentication Package Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves status of Windows security features. Status values except for "Good" should be investigated for potential misconfiguration or security compromise: 
* status - Good, Poor, Snoozed, Not Monitored, Error

osQuery.label: windows_security_features_monitoring
osQuery.name: Windows Security Features Status
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
firewall, autoupdate, antivirus, antispyware, internet_settings, windows_security_center_service,
(SELECT
CASE data
WHEN "1" THEN "Good"
ELSE "Poor"
END uac_status
FROM registry
WHERE
path LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\EnableLUA") AS user_account_control
FROM windows_security_center;

queries.title: Windows Security Features Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. It retrieves Apple System Log events related to Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) service operation which was designed to help users configure the privacy settings of their apps. Hosts where tccd service was restarted or killed should be investigated for suspicious activity:
* timestamp event was recorded on the host
* host - sender's address
* sender - sender's identification
* facility - sender's facility
* level - log level number
* message - message text
* ref_proc - reference process for messages proxied by launchd

osQuery.label: macos_asl_tccd_service_monitoring
osQuery.name: Apple System Log Events
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
DATETIME(time, "unixepoch") AS timestamp,
host, sender, facility, level, message, ref_proc
FROM
asl
WHERE
ref_proc LIKE "%tccd%";

queries.title: Apple System Log tccd Service Events Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. It retrieves data on sharing preferences set up on the host:
  * Screen sharing
  * File sharing
  * Printer sharing
  * Remote login
  * Remote management
  * Remote apple events
  * Internet sharing
  * Bluetooth sharing
  * Disc sharing
  * Content caching

osQuery.label: macos_sharing_preferences_monitoring
osQuery.name: Sharing Preferences Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
CASE screen_sharing WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "screen_sharing_status",
CASE file_sharing WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "file_sharing_status",
CASE printer_sharing WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "printer_sharing_status",
CASE remote_login WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "remote_login_status",
CASE remote_management WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "remote_management_status",
CASE remote_apple_events WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "remote_apple_events_status",
CASE internet_sharing WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "internet_sharing_status",
CASE bluetooth_sharing WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "bluetooth_sharing_status",
CASE disc_sharing WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "disc_sharing_status",
CASE content_caching WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" ELSE "Disabled" END "content_caching_status"
FROM sharing_preferences
WHERE
screen_sharing_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "screen_sharing_value") AND
file_sharing LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "file_sharing_value") AND
printer_sharing_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "printer_sharing_value") AND
remote_login_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "remote_login_value") AND
remote_management_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "remote_management_value") AND
remote_apple_events_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "remote_apple_events_value") AND
internet_sharing_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "internet_sharing_value") AND
bluetooth_sharing_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "bluetooth_sharing_value") AND
disc_sharing_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "disc_sharing_value") AND
content_caching_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "content_caching_value");

queries.title: Sharing Preferences Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves all printer file ports that are registered on the system. Any ports that have a file path in them - especially ending in ".exe" or ".dll" should be examined as they could indicate malware trying to establish persistence on the host or data exfiltration from the system.

osQuery.label: printer_file_ports_monitoring
osQuery.name: Printer File Ports Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, data, datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified 
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Ports"
AND (name LIKE "%:\%" OR name LIKE "%.exe%" OR name LIKE "%.dll%");

queries.title: Suspicious Printer File Ports Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. The OpenSSL versions 3.0.0-3.0.7 and 1.0.2-1.1.1 are vulnerable to multiple critical vulnerabilities.

osQuery.label: windows_openssl_vulnerable_versions
osQuery.name: Windows OpenSSL Vulnerable Versions
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name,
version,
install_location, 
install_date
FROM programs where name like '%openssl%' AND
(((CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 307) AND
(CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) > 300)) OR
((CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 111) AND
(CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) >= 102)) OR
(CAST(SPLIT(CAST(regex_match(name, '(\d.\d.\d)+', 1) as varchar) ,".",0) ||
SPLIT(CAST(regex_match(name, '(\d.\d.\d)+', 1) as varchar) ,".",1) || 
SPLIT(CAST(regex_match(name, '(\d.\d.\d)+', 1) as varchar) ,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 307));

queries.title: Windows OpenSSL Vulnerable Versions Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to MacOS and Linux.  It will find running processes that are tied to binaries with unsual permissions namely 0777.

osQuery.label: unusual_executable_permissions
osQuery.name: Unusual Executable Permissions
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
p.pid,
p.name,
p.path,
f.mode,
hash.sha256,
pp.name AS parent_name,
pp.path AS parent_path,
pp.cmdline AS parent_cmd,
hash.sha256 AS parent_sha256
FROM
processes p
JOIN file f ON p.path = f.path
LEFT JOIN hash ON p.path = hash.path
LEFT JOIN processes pp ON pp.pid = p.parent
WHERE
f.mode NOT IN (
  '0500',
  '0544',
  '0555',
  '0711',
  '0755',
  '0775',
  '6755',
  '0700',
  '2755',
  '4511',
  '4555',
  '4755'
)
AND NOT (
  f.path IN (
    '/Library/Application Support/Logitech/com.logitech.vc.LogiVCCoreService/LogiVCCoreService.app/Contents/MacOS/LogiVCCoreService',
    '/Applications/Camera Settings.app/Contents/MacOS/LogitechCamera'
  )
  AND f.mode = '0777'
  AND f.uid > 500
)
AND NOT (
  f.path LIKE '/Users/%/Library/Application Support/Code/User/globalStorage/grafana.vscode-jsonnet/bin/jsonnet-language-server'
  AND f.mode = '0777'
  AND f.uid > 500
)
AND NOT (
  f.path = '/usr/bin/sudo'
  AND f.mode = '0411'
  AND f.uid = 0
)
AND NOT (
  f.path LIKE '/home/%/.local/share/JetBrains/Toolbox/bin/jetbrains-toolbox'
  AND f.mode = '0744'
);

queries.title: Unusual Executable Permissions Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows.It is designed to capture volatile and non-volatile data from the endpoint that can help with forensics investigations.

osQuery.label: hosts_file
osQuery.name: Hosts File Data

osQuery.label: sha256_hash_of_running_processes
osQuery.name: SHA256 Hash Of Running Processes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT p.pid, p.name, p.path, h.sha256 
FROM processes p INNER JOIN hash h ON p.path=h.path;

osQuery.label: mapped_drives
osQuery.name: Mapped Drives

osQuery.label: interface_names_and_associated_ips
osQuery.name: Interface Names And Associated IPs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT interface, address 
FROM interface_addresses 
WHERE interface NOT LIKE "lo%" 
AND interface!=1;

osQuery.label: process_running_without_a_binary_on_disk
osQuery.name: Process Running Without A Binary On Disk

osQuery.label: shared_resources_monitoring
osQuery.name: Shared Resources
osQuery.sql:
SELECT description, install_date, status, allow_maximum, maximum_allowed, name, path, type
FROM shared_resources;

osQuery.label: application_shims
osQuery.name: Application Shims
osQuery.sql:
SELECT apps.executable, h.sha256, apps.path, apps.description, datetime(apps.install_time,"unixepoch") AS install_time, apps.sdb_id 
FROM appcompat_shims apps LEFT JOIN hash h ON h.path=apps.path;

osQuery.label: logon_sessions_monitoring
osQuery.name: Logon Sessions

osQuery.label: logged_in_users
osQuery.name: Logged In Users

osQuery.label: windows_most_recent_files_monitoring
osQuery.name: Recent Files Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT f.path, f.filename, f.uid, f.gid, f.mode, f.size,
DATETIME(f.atime, "unixepoch") AS last_access_time,
DATETIME(f.mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified,
DATETIME(f.ctime, "unixepoch") AS last_status_change_time,
DATETIME(f.btime, "unixepoch") AS creation_time,
f.hard_links, f.symlink, f.type
FROM file f
WHERE f.path LIKE "C:\Users\%\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Recent\%";

osQuery.label: powershell_history
osQuery.name: PowerShell History
osQuery.sql:
SELECT script_text, datetime
FROM orbital_powershell_events
ORDER BY datetime DESC
LIMIT 500;

osQuery.label: operating_system_attributes
osQuery.name: Operating System Attributes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT platform, name, major, minor, build 
FROM os_version;

osQuery.label: windows_executables_that_automatically_execute
osQuery.name: Windows executables that automatically execute
osQuery.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT ae.name, ae.path, ae.source, h.sha256 
FROM autoexec ae LEFT JOIN hash h ON h.path = ae.path;

osQuery.label: windows_hotfixes
osQuery.name: Windows HotFixes

osQuery.label: listening_ports
osQuery.name: Listening Ports
osQuery.sql:
SELECT p.name, p.path, lp.address, lp.protocol, lp.port 
FROM listening_ports lp LEFT JOIN processes p ON lp.pid = p.pid 
WHERE lp.port != 0 
AND lp.address!="127.0.0.1" 
AND lp.address!="::1" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "c:\windows\system32\%" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "/usr/sbin/%" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "/sbin/%" 
AND p.path NOT LIKE "/usr/libexec/%";

osQuery.label: startup_items
osQuery.name: Startup Items
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, path, args, source, type, status, username 
FROM startup_items;

osQuery.label: installed_programs_on_windows_host
osQuery.name: Installed Programs On Windows Host

osQuery.label: user_accounts
osQuery.name: User Accounts
osQuery.sql:
SELECT uid, gid, username, description, directory, shell, uuid, type 
FROM users;

osQuery.label: windows_prefetch_directory
osQuery.name: Windows Prefetch Directory
osQuery.sql:
SELECT filename, uid, gid, 
datetime(atime, "unixepoch") as last_access_time,
datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") as last_modification_time,
datetime(ctime, "unixepoch") as last_status_change_time, 
datetime(btime, "unixepoch") as create_time,
hard_links, symlink, type, attributes, volume_serial, file_id 
FROM file 
WHERE path LIKE "C:\Windows\Prefetch\%" 
AND size NOT LIKE "0";

osQuery.label: registry_network_shares_monitoring
osQuery.name: Network Registry Key

osQuery.label: connected_networks_history_monitoring
osQuery.name: Network Profiles Registry Key
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\microsoft\windows nt\currentversion\networklist\profiles\%"
AND name IN ("ProfileName", "DateCreated", "DateLastConnected", "Description");

osQuery.label: processes_with_network_connections
osQuery.name: Processes With Network Connections

osQuery.label: user_groups_monitoring
osQuery.name: User Groups
osQuery.sql:
SELECT g.groupname, u.username, g.comment
FROM groups g
LEFT JOIN user_groups ug ON g.gid=ug.gid
LEFT JOIN users u ON ug.uid=u.uid;

osQuery.label: windows_open_shares
osQuery.name: Windows Open Shares

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_hashes_tmp
osQuery.name: Loaded Modules Hashes Temp

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_hashes
osQuery.name: Loaded Modules Hashes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT module_id, sha256, module_path 
FROM loaded_module_hashes;

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_processes
osQuery.name: Processes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT pid, name AS process_name, path AS process_path 
FROM processes;

osQuery.label: loaded_modules_vs_processes
osQuery.name: Loaded Module ID To Process ID
osQuery.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT lh.module_id, pmm.pid AS pid 
FROM process_memory_map pmm JOIN loaded_module_hashes lh ON lh.module_path=pmm.path;

osQuery.label: windows_connected_usb_monitoring
osQuery.name: USBSTOR Registry Key

osQuery.label: scheduled_tasks_monitoring
osQuery.name: Scheduled Tasks
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, action, path, enabled, state, hidden, last_run_time, next_run_time, last_run_message, last_run_code
FROM scheduled_tasks;

osQuery.label: temp_directories_monitoring
osQuery.name: Temp Directory File Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT f.path, f.filename, h.sha256, f.uid, f.gid, f.mode, f.size, 
DATETIME(f.atime, "unixepoch") AS last_access_time, 
DATETIME(f.mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified, 
DATETIME(f.ctime, "unixepoch") AS last_status_change_time, 
DATETIME(f.btime, "unixepoch") AS creation_time,
f.hard_links, f.symlink, f.type
FROM file f LEFT JOIN hash h ON f.path=h.path
WHERE f.path LIKE "C:\Users\%\AppData\Local\Temp\%%";

queries.title: (Deprecated) Forensic Snapshot Windows 0.0.6
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data.

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. The domain name system (DNS) is the way the internet domain names are located and translated into internet protocol (IP) addresses. Malware may attempt to change the DNS setting on the system in order to redirect the user to malicious website. This query returns the DNS search order on the system. All entries should be reviews for any suspicious domain/IP values.

osQuery.label: dns_server_search_order_monitoring
osQuery.name: DNS Server Search Order

queries.title: Rogue DNS Servers Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Link-Local Multicast Name Resolution (LLMNR) And NetBIOS Name Service (NBT-NS) are used as alternate methods of host identification. Malware may attempt the exploit these two services for credential theft and relay attacks. This query retrieves hosts where LLMNR is disabled and monitors for any processes using LLMNR/NetBIOS ports as well as any related Windows Events.

osQuery.label: hosts_with_llmnr_disabled
osQuery.name: LLMNR Disabled
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\DNSClient"
AND name LIKE "EnableMulticast"
AND data="0";

osQuery.label: hosts_with_llmnt_traffic
osQuery.name: LLMNR Traffic
osQuery.sql:
SELECT p.name, lp.address, lp.port
FROM processes p
JOIN listening_ports lp ON lp.pid=p.pid
WHERE lp.port IN (5355);

osQuery.label: llmnr_windows_events
osQuery.name: LLMNR Windows Events
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime AS TimeGenerated,
data AS Message,
eventid AS EventIdentifier,
channel AS Logfile,
provider_name AS SourceName,
"N/A" AS Type,
"N/A" AS TimeWritten
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE (
channel = "System" AND eventid = 7045) OR (
channel = "Security" AND eventid = 4697);

queries.title: LLMNR Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It performs a YARA scan that searches binaries of running processes for WMI commands:
* path - the path scanned
* matches - list of YARA matches
* count - number of YARA matches
* strings - matching strings
* tags - matching tags
* sha256 of the scanned binary

osQuery.label: windows_yara_binary_contains_wmi_command
osQuery.name: YARA Scan Results
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
y.path, y.matches, y.count,
y.strings, y.tags,
h.sha256
FROM yara y
LEFT JOIN hash h ON h.path=y.path
WHERE
y.path IN (SELECT DISTINCT(path) FROM processes WHERE path!="" AND path LIKE "C:\Users\%") AND
y.sigrule = 'rule pe_wmi_cmd : anomaly { strings: $wmi = /select (\*|.{1,20}) from win32_/ ascii wide nocase condition: $wmi }' AND
y.count != 0;

queries.title: YARA Scan: Binary Contains a WMI command

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. If the path to a service is not wrapped in quotes and there are spaces in the path - it is possible to abuse this service to escalate privileges or to gain execution on the system. In addition, a malware can create a service that intentionally introduces this vulnerability. This query retrieves such services. The results should be reviewed for any suspicious entries. The service path should be split at each space, to generate sub-paths and each such sub-path should be inspected for presence of an executable.

osQuery.label: unquoted_service_paths_monitoring
osQuery.name: Service With Unquoted Paths
osQuery.sql:
SELECT s.name, s.service_type, s.pid, s.status, s.start_type, s.path, s.module_path, s.user_account, h.sha256 
FROM services s LEFT JOIN hash h ON s.path=h.path 
WHERE s.path NOT LIKE '"%"' 
AND s.path LIKE "% %" 
AND s.path LIKE "%.exe";

queries.title: Unquoted Service Paths Monitoring
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It returns signing information for the files based on the provided pattern for the file path:
* path - file path or directory
* original_program_name - the original program name that the publisher has signed
* serial_number - the certificate serial number
* issuer_name - the certificate issuer name
* subject_name - the certificate subject name
* result - the signature check result

osQuery.label: check_file_code_signing_status
osQuery.name: File Code Signing Status

queries.title: Check File Code Signing Status

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It presents information about powershell executions on the host.

osQuery.label: powershell_events
osQuery.name: Powershell Events
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
script_name, script_path, script_text, script_block_id, script_block_count 
FROM orbital_powershell_events
WHERE
script_text LIKE regex_match(script_text, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "script_text_pattern"), 0) AND
script_path LIKE regex_match(script_path, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "script_path_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Powershell Events Monitoring
warnings: This query requires PowerShell Script Block Logging enabled.
This query could produce a large volume of results.

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It searches for events where account was detected updating its own group membership which is not a normal activity inside the domain environment and could indicate an adversary attempting to establish persistence on the host.

osQuery.label: windows_eventlogs_account_updated_its_group_membership
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
regex_match(data, 'MemberName":"([^"]+)', 1) as member_name,
regex_match(data, 'MemberSid":"([^"]+)', 1) as member_sid,
regex_match(data, 'TargetUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) as target_group,
regex_match(data, 'TargetDomainName":"([^"]+)', 1) as target_group_domain,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) as subject_user_name,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectDomainName":"([^"]+)', 1) as subject_domain_name,       
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4728 AND
member_name LIKE '%' || subject_user_name || '%';

queries.title: Windows Event Logs: Account Updated Its Own Group Membership

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Logon scripts are launched when the user logs into the system and originally designed for administrative purposes. Malware authors may attempt to modify the logon scripts in order to maintain persistence on the host or to move laterally within a network. This query retrieves data from registry on logon scripts set up on the system.

osQuery.label: logon_scripts_monitoring
osQuery.name: Logon Scripts Regisry Keys
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\Environment"
AND name IN ("UserInitLogonServer", "UserInitLogonScript", "UserInitMprLogonScript");

queries.title: Windows Logon Scripts Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. Kernel extension mechanism allows dynamic loading of code into the kernel, without the need to recompile or relink. This query monitors for kernel extensions on the host: 
* idx - extension load tag or index
* refs - reference count
* size - bytes of wired memory used by extension
* name - extension label
* version - extension version
* linked_against - indexes of extensions this extension is linked against
* optional path to extension bundle
Search suggestion:
* To find all extensions that were not written by Apple set parameter "name_pattern" to "^((?!com.apple).)*$"

osQuery.label: macos_kernel_extensions_monitoring
osQuery.name: Kernel Extensions
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
idx, refs, size, name, version, linked_against, path
FROM kernel_extensions
WHERE 
name LIKE regex_match(name, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "name_pattern"), 0)
ORDER BY name;

queries.title: Kernel Extensions Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Network Provider registry entries are used to instruct Windows on how to handle proprietary protocols. These registry keys associate a network protocol with the provider DLL and dictate the order of use. Modifications to these registry entries could indicate an attempt by malware to maintain persistence on the system. This query retrieves data related to these registry entries.

osQuery.label: networkprovider_registry
osQuery.name: Networkprovider Registry
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data,  datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE (key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\%\NetworkProvider"
OR key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\ControlSet002\Services\%\NetworkProvider")
AND name LIKE "ProviderPath";

queries.title: Networkprovider Registry Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. "CVE-2020-1472" is a privilege elevation vulnerability in the Netlogon Remote Protocol (MS-NRPC). This query checks if servers are vulnerable to this CVE by:
* Checking if correct patches mitigating "CVE-2020-1472" were installed on the server. For different Server version different patches  should be applied:
  * Server 2008 R2 x64 SP 1 - "4571729"
  * Server 2008 R2 x64 SP1 Core - "4571729"
  * Windows Server 2012 Core - "4571736" OR "4571702"
  * Windows Server 2012 - "4571736" OR "4571702"
  * Windows Server 2012 R2 - "4571703" OR "4571723", superseded by "KB4577066"
  * Windows Server 2012 R2 Core - "4571703" OR "4571723", superseded by "KB4577066"
  * Windows Server 2016 - "4571694"
  * Windows Server 2016 Core - "4571694"
  * Windows Server 2019 - "4565349"
  * Windows Server 2019 Core - "4565349"
  * Windows Server 1903 Core - "4565351"
  * Windows Server 1909 Core - "4565351"
  * Windows Server 2004 Core - "4566782"
* Checking if "Enforcement mode" preventing vulnerable Netlogon connections is set on the host:
  * Value of "1" indicates that the mitigation is in place
  * Value of "0" or non-existing key indicates that the mitigation was not applied
  * this registry key will not be supported once February 9, 2021 patch is applied

osQuery.label: windows_cve_2020_1472_patches
osQuery.name: CVE-2020-1472 Patches Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT csname, hotfix_id, caption, installed_by, installed_on 
FROM patches
WHERE hotfix_id IN 
("KB4577066", "KB4571729", "KB4571736", "KB4571702", "KB4571703", "KB4571723", "KB4571694", "KB4565349", "KB4565351", "KB4566782");

osQuery.label: windows_cve_2020_1472_registry
osQuery.name: Enforcement Mode Registry Key Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified,
CASE data
WHEN "1" THEN "Not Vulnerable"
ELSE "Vulnerable"
END "CVE-2020-1472 Status"
FROM registry
WHERE path LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters\FullSecureChannelProtection";

queries.title: CVE-2020-1472 Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It lists the set of root/system processes which are making a non-local-host network connection or listening. The following data is retrieved:
* process name
* process command line
* process id
* process remote ip address
* process remote port 
* process local ip address
* process local port

osQuery.label: privileged_processes_with_non-localhost_network_connections
osQuery.name: Privileged Processes With Non-localhost Network Connections
osQuery.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT p.name, p.cmdline, pos.pid, pos.local_address, pos.local_port, pos.remote_address, pos.remote_port 
FROM processes p JOIN process_open_sockets pos USING (pid) 
WHERE pos.remote_address NOT IN ("", "0.0.0.0", "127.0.0.1", "::", "::1") 
AND p.uid=0;

queries.title: Privileged Processes With Non-localhost Network Connections

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. CVE-2022-30190 (Follina) is a zero day vulnerability in Microsoft Support Diagnostic Tool (MSDT) allowing remote code execution on the host. The temporary mitigation against it is to remove the "ms-msdt" registry key or to enable the "Scripted Diagnostics Set" group policy. This query checks if temporary mitigations against Follina are present on the host:
* All hosts where "EnableDiagnostics" registry key is not present or not set to "0" could be vulnerable to CVE-2022-30190
* If "EnableDiagnostics" registry key is missing from the host but "ms-msdt" registry key is present - the host is vulnerable to CVE-2022-30190

osQuery.label: windows_registry_follina_mitigation_msdt_protocol
osQuery.name: Registry
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE
path LIKE "HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\ms-msdt\%";

osQuery.label: windows_registry_follina_mitigation_msdt_group_policy
osQuery.name: Registry
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE
path LIKE 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\ScriptedDiagnostics\EnableDiagnostics' AND
data!="0";

queries.title: Windows CVE-2022-30190 Registry Mitigations

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. The assessment report was introduced with Windows Vista and it examines a number of hardware components to determine an overall score for the system. This query retrieves data from the assessment report. This data can be used in monitoring overall system performance and its components. Data displayed are:
* CPUScore - a score for the processors on the system
* DiskScore - a score for the sequential read throughput on the primary hard disk on the system
* GraphicsScore - a score for the graphics capabilities of the system
* MemoryScore - a score for the memory throughput and capacity of the system
* WinSATAssessmentState - state of the assessment
* WinSPRLevel - base score for the system

osQuery.label: system_assessment_report_monitoring
osQuery.name: System Assessment Report
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
PRINTF("%.2f", CPUScore) AS "CPU Score",
PRINTF("%.2f", DiskScore) AS "Disk Score",
PRINTF("%.2f", GraphicsScore) AS "Graphics Score",
PRINTF("%.2f", MemoryScore) AS "Memory Score",
CASE WinSATAssessmentState
WHEN "0" THEN "State Unknown"
WHEN "1" THEN "Valid"
WHEN "2" THEN "IncoherentWithHardware"
WHEN "3" THEN "NoAssessmentAvailable"
WHEN "4" THEN "Invalid"
END "WinSAT Assessment State",
PRINTF("%.2f", WinSPRLevel) AS "WinSPR Level"
FROM Win32_WinSat;

queries.title: System Assessment Report Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. Windows event log with ID "5137" is generated when an Active Directory object is created. This query collects events related to a new server registration which might indicate an adversary creating a rogue Domain Controller:
* datetime - timestamp when the event was generated
* subject_user_sid - a security identifier of account that requested the "create object" operation
* subject_user_name - the name of the account that requested the "create object" operation
* subject_domain_name - subject's domain or computer name
* object_class - class of the object that was created
* data - full event data

osQuery.label: windows_eventlogs_directory_service_object_created
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
regex_match(data, '"SubjectUserSid":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_user_sid,
regex_match(data, '"SubjectUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_user_name,
regex_match(data, '"SubjectDomainName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_domain_name,
regex_match(data, '"ObjectClass":"([^"]+)', 1) AS object_class,
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 5137 AND
object_class LIKE "server";

queries.title: Windows Event Logs: A Server Directory Service Object Created

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves Windows event log entries related to account access removal. The event IDs monitored are listed below:
* 4723 - an attempt was made to change an account's password
* 4724 - an attempt was made to reset an account's password
* 4726 - a user account was deleted
* 4740 - a user account was locked out

osQuery.label: event_logs_account_access_removal_monitoring
osQuery.name: Account Access Removal Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
data AS Message,
datetime AS "Time Generated",
eventid,
channel AS LogFile,
provider_name AS SourceName
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid IN (4723, 4724, 4726, 4740);

queries.title: Windows Event Logs For Account Access Removal Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. The Trusted Platform Module (TPM) chip is designed to provide hardware-based, security-related functions. This query retrieves information about the TMP chip installed on the system. The following data is retrieved:
* IsActivated_InitialValue - True if the device is activated. Otherwise, False.
* IsEnabled_InitialValue - True if the device is enabled. Otherwise, False.
* IsOwned_InitialValue - True if the device has the owner. Otherwise False.
* SpecVersion - the version of the Trusted Computing Group (TCG) specification that the TPM supports.
* ManufacturerVersion - the version of the TPM, as specified by the manufacturer.
* ManufacturerVersionInfo - other manufacturer-specific version information for the TPM.
* ManufacturerId - the identifying information that uniquely names the TPM manufacturer.
* PhysicalPresenceVersionInfo - the version of the Physical Presence Interface, a communication mechanism used to run device operations that require physical presence, that the computer supports.
** 1 means True, 0 means False

osQuery.label: windows_tmp_monitoring
osQuery.name: Trusted Platform Module Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT IsActivated_InitialValue, IsEnabled_InitialValue, IsOwned_InitialValue, SpecVersion, ManufacturerVersion, ManufacturerVersionInfo, ManufacturerId, PhysicalPresenceVersionInfo
FROM Win32_Tpm;

queries.title: Windows Trusted Platform Module Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. The Windows Installer APIs properties, which derive from the Windows Installer binary, allow a product to configure its uninstall properties which will be visible from the Control Panel. These uninstall properties are stored in the registry and could be used to execute malicious code on the host when the user needs to uninstall specific application through control panel. This query retrieves such uninstall entries from the registry. Collected data should be reviewed to prevent malicious execution.

osQuery.label: windows_registry_uninstall_entries
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
key, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified 
FROM registry 
WHERE 
path IN (
SELECT path || "\UninstallString" 
FROM registry 
WHERE 
key LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall") AND
data LIKE regex_match(data, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "data_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Registry Uninstall Entries Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Applocker is used by administrators to allow specific users or groups to run certain applications, while denying access to others. The policy settings for Applocker are stored under SrpV2 registry key. Malware may attempt to modify this registry key to allow an execution of specific file types from specific directories. This query monitors any modifications applied to SrpV2 registry key.

osQuery.label: applocker_registry
osQuery.name: Applocker Registry

queries.title: Applocker Registry Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. The Script block logging feature logs blocks of code as they are executed by the PowerShell engine. Malware authors will often attempt to disable it in order to cover their tracks to avoid forensic analysis and detection. This query monitors for any scripts that attempt to disable the script block logging on the system.

osQuery.label: script_block_logging_disabled
osQuery.name: Script Block Logging Disabled
osQuery.sql:
SELECT datetime, time, script_block_count, script_block_id, script_name, script_path, script_text 
FROM orbital_powershell_events 
WHERE script_text LIKE '%System.Management.Automation.AmsiUtils%amsiInitFailed%SetValue($null,$true)%' 
OR script_text LIKE "%Remove%EnableScriptBlockLogging%";

queries.title: Script Block Logging Disabled

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves information on recently opened Office documents:
* application - associated Office application
* version - office application version number
* file path
* most recent time file was opened
* user SID of the user who opened the document
* username of the user who opened the document
* sha256 for the Office document

osQuery.label: windows_office_mru_monitoring
osQuery.name: Recently Opened Office Documents
osQuery.sql:
SELECT                                                                                                         
om.application, om.version, om.path,                                                                           
DATETIME(om.last_opened_time, "unixepoch") AS last_opened_time,                                         
om.sid, u.username, h.sha256                                                                                   
FROM                                                                                                           
office_mru om                                                                                                  
LEFT JOIN hash h ON h.path=om.path                                                                             
JOIN users u ON u.uuid=om.sid
WHERE
om.path LIKE regex_match(om.path, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "path_pattern"), 0) AND
u.username LIKE regex_match(u.username, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "username_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Recently Opened Office Documents Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It retrieves information on all users currently logged into the system: 
* user - user name
* login type - active, disconnected, connected, connectquery, shadow, idle, listen, reset, down, init
* device name
* remote hostname 
* time entry was made 
* process ID 
* user's security identifier

osQuery.label: logged_in_users
osQuery.name: Logged In Users
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
user, type as "login_type", tty as "device_name", 
host as "remote_hostname", DATETIME(time, "unixepoch") AS time, 
pid, sid  
FROM logged_in_users
WHERE 
user!="";

queries.title: Logged In Users

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves LOLBins execution events from Windows EventLogs' channel "Security" and eventid "4688" which is for new process creation event. Following data is retrieved:
* datetime - Date and time of the event as stated in eventlogs
* new_process_name - Newly created process name e.g certutil.exe
* new_process_path - Full path of the new process
* parent_process_name - Parent process name only e.g. cmd.exe
* parent_process_path - Full path of the new process
* new_process_commandline - New process commandline
* data - Eventlog data

osQuery.label: windows_lolbin_execution_event
osQuery.name: Windows LOLBin Execution Event
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
datetime,
LOWER(replace(regex_match(data, 'NewProcessName":"([^"]+)\\([^"]+)', 2), "\\", "\")) AS new_process_name,
replace(regex_match(data, 'NewProcessName":"([^"]+)', 1), "\\", "\") AS new_process_path, 
LOWER(replace(regex_match(data, 'ParentProcessName":"([^"]+)\\([^"]+)', 2), "\\", "\")) AS parent_process_name,
replace(regex_match(data, 'ParentProcessName":"([^"]+)', 1), "\\", "\") AS parent_process_path,
replace(regex_match(data, 'CommandLine":"(.+?)","', 1), "\\", "\") AS new_process_commandline,
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4688 AND
parent_process_name NOT IN ("svchost.exe", "dllhost.exe") AND 
new_process_name IN ("bash.exe", "bitsadmin.exe", "certutil.exe", "cmdkey.exe", "cmstp.exe", "control.exe", "csc.exe", "dfsvc.exe", "diskshadow.exe", "dnscmd.exe", "esentutl.exe", "eventvwr.exe", "expand.exe", "extexport.exe", "extrac32.exe", "findstr.exe", "forfiles.exe", "ftp.exe", "gpscript.exe", "hh.exe", "ie4uinit.exe", "ieexec.exe", "infdefaultinstall.exe", "installutil.exe", "makecab.exe", "mavinject.exe", "microsoft.workflow.compiler.exe", "mmc.exe", "msbuild.exe", "msconfig.exe", "msdt.exe", "mshta.exe", "msiexec.exe", "odbcconf.exe", "pcalua.exe", "pcwrun.exe", "presentationhost.exe", "print.exe", "reg.exe", "regasm.exe", "regedit.exe", "register-cimprovider.exe", "regsvcs.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "replace.exe", "rpcping.exe", "rundll32.exe", "runonce.exe", "runscripthelper.exe", "sc.exe", "schtasks.exe", "scriptrunner.exe", "syncappvpublishingserver.exe", "verclsid.exe", "wab.exe", "wmic.exe", "wscript.exe", "xwizard.exe", "appvlp.exe", "bginfo.exe", "cdb.exe", "csi.exe", "dnx.exe", "dxcap.exe", "mftrace.exe", "msdeploy.exe", "msxsl.exe", "rcsi.exe", "sqldumper.exe", "sqlps.exe", "sqltoolsps.exe", "te.exe", "tracker.exe", "vsjitdebugger.exe");

queries.title: Windows LOLBins Execution

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves data on Microsoft Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE) child processes. EQNEDT32.exe is used by Microsoft Office and Wordpad to allow for math functions in documents, therefore it is highly suspicious when this process creates child processes and could be associated with exploits for vulnerabilities such as CVE-2017-11882 and CVE-2018-0802, which in turn leverage a vulnerability in Microsoft Equation Editor. The following data is retrieved:
* process id
* process name
* path to the executed process binary
* process command line 
* process state
* process current working directory
* process user id 
* on disk will be set to 1 if the process path exists, 0 is no, and -1 if unknown 
* executed process binary's total size
* elevated token will be set to 1 if the process uses elevated token, 0 if no
* process handle count

osQuery.label: eqnedt32_child_processes_monitoring_tmp
osQuery.name: EQNEDT32.exe Child Processes Temp
osQuery.sql:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE parent_child AS
SELECT child_processes.pid AS child_pid,
child_processes.name AS child_name,
parent_processes.pid AS parent_pid,
parent_processes.name AS parent_name
FROM processes child_processes INNER JOIN processes parent_processes
ON child_processes.parent=parent_processes.pid;

osQuery.label: eqnedt32_child_processes_monitoring
osQuery.name: EQNEDT32.exe Child Processes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
p.pid, p.name, p.path, p.cmdline, p.state, p.cwd, p.uid, p.on_disk, p.total_size, p.is_elevated_token, p.handle_count
FROM processes p
WHERE 
p.pid IN (
SELECT child_pid 
FROM parent_child 
WHERE parent_name LIKE "eqnedt32.exe");

queries.title: Microsoft Equation Editor Child Processes Monitoring

starting490.label: eqnedt32_child_processes_monitoring_tmp
starting490.name: EQNEDT32.exe Child Processes Temp
starting490.sql:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE parent_child AS
SELECT child_processes.pid AS child_pid,
child_processes.name AS child_name,
parent_processes.pid AS parent_pid,
parent_processes.name AS parent_name
FROM processes child_processes INNER JOIN processes parent_processes
ON child_processes.parent=parent_processes.pid;

starting490.label: eqnedt32_child_processes_monitoring
starting490.name: EQNEDT32.exe Child Processes
starting490.sql:
SELECT 
p.pid, p.name, p.path, p.cmdline, p.state, p.cwd, p.uid, p.on_disk, p.total_size, p.elevated_token AS is_elevated_token, p.handle_count
FROM processes p
WHERE 
p.pid IN (
SELECT child_pid 
FROM parent_child 
WHERE parent_name LIKE "eqnedt32.exe");

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Malware could modify winsock parameters to enable automatic loading of malicious code. This query retrieves data related to winsock settings in registry.

osQuery.label: winsock_parameters
osQuery.name: Winsock Parameters
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE (key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\WinSock2\Parameters" AND type NOT LIKE "subkey")
OR (key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\WinSock2\%%" AND name IN ("AppFullPath", "LibraryPath", "ProtocolName"))
OR (key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\ControlSet002\Services\WinSock2\Parameters" AND type NOT LIKE "subkey")
OR (key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\ControlSet002\Services\WinSock2\%%" AND name IN ("AppFullPath", "LibraryPath", "ProtocolName"));

queries.title: Winsock Parameters Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Linux. The OpenSSL versions 3.0.0-3.0.7 and 1.0.2-1.1.1 are vulnerable to multiple critical vulnerabilities.

osQuery.label: linux_openssl_vulnerable_versions_deb
osQuery.name: Linux OpenSSL Vulnerable Versions (DEB)
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name,
version
FROM deb_packages
WHERE name LIKE '%openssl%' AND
(((CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 307) AND
(CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) > 300)) OR
((CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 111) AND
(CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) > 101))) ;

osQuery.label: openssl_vulnerable_versions_linux_rpm
osQuery.name: Linux Vulnerable OpenSSL Versions (RPM)
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name,
version
FROM rpm_packages
WHERE name LIKE '%openssl%' AND
(((CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 307) AND
(CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) > 300)) OR
((CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) < 111) AND
(CAST(SPLIT(version,".",0) || SPLIT(version,".",1) || SPLIT(version,".", 2) AS INTEGER) > 101))) ;

osQuery.label: linux_openssl_vulnerable_versions_yara
osQuery.name: Linux OpenSSL Vulnerable Versions in Non-System Binaries Folders
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
(path LIKE "/opt/local/bin/%" OR path LIKE "/usr/local/bin/%") AND
sigrule='rule openssl_vulnerable_versions { meta: author = "Talos" strings: $s1 = /OpenSSL(\s)?3\.[0-9]{1}.[0-6]{1}/ nocase ascii $s2 = /OpenSSL(\s)?[(0-1)]{1}\.[0-9]{1}.[(0|8|2)]{1}/ nocase ascii condition: any of them}' AND  
matches!="";

queries.title: Linux OpenSSL Vulnerable Versions Monitoring
warnings: This query runs yara scan on some common paths to mitigate resource usage. You can run it manually and change the paths according to your environment.

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. A shim is a small library which transparently intercepts an API, changes the parameters passed, handles the operation itself, or redirects the operation elsewhere. Shims can also be used for running programs on different software platforms than they were developed for. While shims normally serve a legitimate purpose, they can be used in a malicious manner such as: argument replacement, shellcode injection, and others. This query returns information on shims installed on the system based on shim name, database path or installed time. Shims are usually installed using sdbinst.exe, which places the file in a path similar to 'C:\Windows\AppPath%'. Shims installed in non-default path should be considered suspicious.

osQuery.label: application_compatibility_shims_search
osQuery.name: Application Compatibility Shims
osQuery.sql:
SELECT apps.executable, apps.path, apps.description, datetime(apps.install_time, "unixepoch") as installation_time, apps.sdb_id, hash.sha256, users.username
FROM appcompat_shims apps
JOIN file ON file.path=apps.path
JOIN users ON file.uid=users.uid
JOIN hash ON hash.path=apps.path
WHERE apps.executable LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="exe_shim_name")
AND apps.path LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="shim_sdb_path")
AND installation_time LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="shim_install_time");

queries.title: Application Compatibility Shims Search

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. Windows event "4625" is generated when a failed logon attempt is detected. Such events could indicate potential brute forcing, password spraying or password cracking attacks and should always be investigated for suspicious activity:
* datetime - time event was generated
* subject_user_name - the name of the account that reported information about logon failure
* target_user_name - the name of the account that was specified in the logon attempt
* status_code - the reason why logon failed
* logon_type - the type of logon that was performed
* process_name - full path and the name of the executable for the process
* ip_port - ip address and port of machine from which logon attempt was performed
* data - full event data
Logon Types descriptions:
* Logon type 2 - local user logon
* Logon type 3 - logon from the network (access to shared resources, printers, etc.)
* Logon type 4 - batch logon type. This logon type is commonly used by batch servers, where processes may be executing on behalf of a user without their direct intervention
* Logon type 5 - service logon
* Logon type 7 - unlocking password protected screen
* Logon type 8 - logon with clear text credentials passed on the network
* Logon type 9 - logon with new credentials specified for the outbound connections
* Logon type 10 - logon from a remote machine using RDP
* Logon type 11 - cached interactive logon when DC was not contacted to verify supplied credentials

osQuery.label: windows_eventlogs_failed_logons_monitoring
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
regex_match(data, '"SubjectUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_user_name,
regex_match(data, '"TargetUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS target_user_name,
CASE
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC000006D" THEN "Bad username or authentication information."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC000005E" THEN "Currently no logon servers available to service the logon request."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0xC0000064" THEN "Misspelled or bad user account."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0xC000006A" THEN "Misspelled or bad user account."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC000006E" THEN "Valid credentials. Some user account restriction has prevented successful authentication."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0xC000006F" THEN "Outside authorized hours."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0xC0000070" THEN "Unauthorized workstation."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0xC0000071" THEN "Expired password."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0xC0000072" THEN "Account disabled by administrator."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC00000DC" THEN "Sam Server state error."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC0000133" THEN "Clocks out of sync."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC000015B" THEN "The user has not been granted the requested logon type."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC000018C" THEN "Trust relationship between the primary domain and the trusted domain failed."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC0000192" THEN "Netlogon service was not started."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0xC0000193" THEN "Expired account."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC0000224" THEN "User is required to change password at next logon."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0xC0000234" THEN "Account locked."
WHEN regex_match(data, '"Status":"([^"]+)', 1)  LIKE "0XC0000413" THEN "The machine you are logging on to is protected by an authentication firewall."
ELSE "Status code is unknown."
END status_code, 
regex_match(data, '"LogonType":"([^"]+)', 1) AS logon_type,
regex_match(data, '"ProcessName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS process_name,
(regex_match(data, '"IpAddress":"([^"]+)', 1) || ":" || regex_match(data, '"IpPort":"([^"]+)', 1)) AS ip_port,
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4625 AND
logon_type LIKE regex_match(logon_type, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="logon_type_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Windows Event Logs: Failed Logons Monitoring
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. It retrieves the extended attributes of files. Extended attributes are arbitrary file metadata that are separate from the filesystem attributes (eg. file size and permissions) and the file's contents. Adversaries may hide malicious data within a file's extended attributes. This query displays:
* path - Path of the file
* directory - Directory of the file
* key - Extended attribute key
* value - Extended attribute value
* base64 - If the displayed value has been base64 encoded

osQuery.label: macos_extended_attributes
osQuery.name: Extended Attributes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT path, directory, key, value,
CASE base64 WHEN "1" THEN "True" ELSE "False" END "Field Value Base64 Encoded"
FROM extended_attributes 
WHERE
path LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "path_pattern") AND
directory LIKE (SELECT v from __vars WHERE n = "directory_pattern") AND
key LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "key_pattern") AND
value LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "value_pattern")
GROUP BY path, key, value;

queries.title: Extended Attributes
warnings: This query can result in excessive amounts of data. Additional conditional clauses for reducing data size and increasing relevance are advised.

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. The Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) is a system firmware that operates as the software interface between the operating system and hardware of the system. Secure Boot is used to force the system firmware to check that the system boot loader is signed with a cryptographic key authorized by a database contained in the firmware. Malware could attempt to disable Secure Boot in order to establish persistence on the host and prevent its removal.

osQuery.label: secure_boot_status_monitoring
osQuery.name: Secure Boot Status Registry Key
osQuery.sql:
SELECT name, data, datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified,
CASE data WHEN "0" THEN "Disabled" WHEN "1" THEN "Enabled" END "Secure Boot Status"
FROM registry 
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecureBoot\State"
AND name LIKE "UEFISecureBootEnabled";

queries.title: Secure Boot Status Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. PowerShell Empire is a post-exploitation framework used by pentesters, red teams and malicious actors. This query is designed to detect the presence of the default Command and Control code used in PowerShell Empire scripts.

osQuery.label: powershell_empire_default_cnc
osQuery.name: Powershell Empire Default CnC
osQuery.sql:
SELECT datetime, time, script_block_count, script_block_id, script_name, script_path, script_text 
FROM orbital_powershell_events 
WHERE script_text LIKE "%headers.add%user-agent%" 
AND script_text LIKE "%mozilla%" 
AND script_text LIKE "%/login/process.php%";

queries.title: Powershell Empire Default CnC

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. User Access Control (UAC) is a Windows feature designed to prevent unauthorized changed on the system. The "ConsentPromptBehaviorUser" registry key defines the UAC behaviour for standard users. This key should be set to 1 or 3 to turn off the UAC privilege escalation for standard users. The "EnableInstallerDetection" registry key determines if application installations prompt for elevation or not. This key should be set to 1 which will require credential for the installation and log the installation attempt as well.

osQuery.label: windows_uac_prompt_behavior_monitoring
osQuery.name: UAC Prompt Registry Key
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System"
AND name IN ("ConsentPromptBehaviorUser", "EnableInstallerDetection");

queries.title: Windowd User Acess Control Prompt Behavior Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. Windows Credentials Guards is a security feature which only allows trusted, privileged applications and processes to access user secrets, or credentials. This query retrieves registry keys that configure Windows Credentials Guard on the host:
* Registry key "EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity" -  0 disables Credentials Guard
* Registry key "RequirePlatformSecurityFeatures" - 1 is for user secure boot only, 3 is for secure boot and DMA
* Registry key "LsaCfgFlags" - 1 is to enable Credentials Guard with UEFI lock, 2 - enable without the lock, 0 - disable

osQuery.label: credentials_guard_registry_configuration
osQuery.name: Credentials Guard Registry Configuration
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE (
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\DeviceGuard" 
AND (name LIKE "EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity" OR name LIKE "RequirePlatformSecurityFeatures"))
OR (
key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\system\currentcontrolset\control\lsa"
AND name LIKE "LsaCfgFlags");

queries.title: Credentials Guard Registry Configuration

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. The hosts file is the local host database which is checked before a name resolution request is sent to a DNS server. A host entry consists of a hostname, and it's corresponding IP address. It is often used by the malware authors to redirect traffic from the intended destination to sites hosting malicious or unwanted content. It may also be used to block legitimate content such as AV signature updates. On the other hand, it can be used legitimately, and this query may need to be customized to exclude legitimate entries.

osQuery.label: hosts_file
osQuery.name: Hosts File Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
address, hostnames 
FROM etc_hosts 
WHERE 
hostnames NOT IN ("localhost", "::1", "fe00::0", "ff00::0", "ff02::1", "ff02::2");

queries.title: Hosts File Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. DCSync is an attack that allows an attacker to simulate the behaviour of Domain Controller (DC) in order to retrieve password data via domain replication. Once an attacker has access to a privileged account with domain replication rights, the attacker can utilize protocols to mimic a domain controller. Windows Events with ID "4662" indicate an event generated every time an operation was performed on an Active Directory, "Access Mask" value of "0x100" indicates control access, meaning that access allowed only after extended rights checks supported by the object are performed. The "Properties" value of "1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2", "1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2" or "89e95b76-444d-4c62-991a-0facbeda640c" indicate:
* "89e95b76-444d-4c62-991a-0facbeda640c" DS-Replication-Get-Changes-In-Filtered-Set extended right
* "1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2" DS-Replication-Get-Changes extended right
* "1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2" DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All extended right
The account name ending with "$" indicates a machine account and such activity is normal for Active Directory Domain Services since Domain Controllers are responsible for the replication. However, systems where such Windows Events logs are observed and generated by a non-machine account should be investigated for potential compromise.

osQuery.label: windows_dcsync_non_machine_account_monitoring
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
data AS Message,
regex_match(data, '"Properties":"([^"]+)', 1) AS Access_Type,
datetime AS "Time Generated",
channel AS LogFile,
provider_name AS SourceName
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4662 AND
data LIKE '"AccessMask":"0x100"' AND (
Access_Type LIKE "%89e95b76-444d-4c62-991a-0facbeda640c%" OR
Access_Type LIKE "%1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2%" OR 
Access_Type LIKE "%1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2%") AND
datetime LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "datetime_pattern");

queries.title: Active Directory Replication from Non Machine Account Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It collects Windows event logs related to account creation/modification with the "Don't Expire Password" option enabled. This configuration could be abused by the attackers for persistence:
* datetime the event was generated
* subject_user_name - the name of the account that requested the "change user account" operation
* subject_user_sid - subject's domain or computer name
* target_user_name - the name of the account that was changed
* target_sid - SID of account that was changed
* user_account_control - shows the list of changes in userAccountControl attribute
* data - full event log data

osQuery.label: windows_eventlogs_dontexpirepassword_account
osQuery.name: Windows Event Logs
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
datetime,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_user_name,
regex_match(data, 'SubjectUserSid":"([^"]+)', 1) AS subject_user_sid,
regex_match(data, 'TargetUserName":"([^"]+)', 1) AS target_user_name,
regex_match(data, 'TargetSid":"([^"]+)', 1) AS target_sid,
regex_match(data, 'UserAccountControl":"([^"]+)', 1) AS user_account_control,
data
FROM windows_eventlog
WHERE
channel = "Security" AND
eventid = 4738 AND
user_account_control LIKE "%2057%";

queries.title: Windows Event Logs: Accounts Without Password Expiration

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves data on registry settings required for enabled PowerShell Event Auditing.

osQuery.label: powershell_event_auditing_state
osQuery.name: PowerShell Events Audit Registry Settings
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") as last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\%"
OR key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\%";

queries.title: PowerShell Event Auditing State Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It collects data from the "Win32_PowerPlan" WMI class representing a power plan on the system, which consists of a group of power settings and preference information:
* description - the description of the power plan
* elementname - the friendly name of the power plan
* isactive - indicates whether the power scheme is currently active on the system

osQuery.label: windows_wmi_powerplan
osQuery.name: Win32_PowerPlan
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
description, elementname, 
CASE isactive
WHEN "1" THEN "True"
ELSE "False"
END is_active
FROM Win32_PowerPlan;

queries.title: Windows WMI Power Plan Schemes

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. It retrieves file code signing status for the specified file path. Data displayed:
* path - must be provided for search to work 
* arch - if applicable, the  arch of the signed code
* signed - if file is signed
* identifier - the signing identifier sealed into the signature
* team_identifier - the team signing identifier sealed into the signature
* authority - Certificate Common Name

osQuery.label: macos_signature_monitoring
osQuery.name: File Code Signing Status
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, arch,
CASE signed WHEN "1" THEN "Signed" ELSE "Not signed" END "signed_status",
identifier, team_identifier, authority
FROM signature
WHERE
path LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "path_pattern") AND
signed_status LIKE (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "signed_pattern");

queries.title: File Code Signing Status Monitoring

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. Server Message Block (SMB) is a network communication protocol for providing shared access to files, printers, and serial ports between nodes on a network. The oldest version of SMB protocol, SMBv1 is highly insecure and should be disabled on the host. Malware such as WannaCry, NotPetya and others often exploits SMBv1 vulnerabilities to gain remote code execution on the host, or to propagate and infect over systems in the target environment.

osQuery.label: smbv1_enabled_monitoring
osQuery.name: SMBv1 Registry Key
osQuery.sql:
SELECT key AS reg_key, path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\system\currentcontrolset\services\lanmanserver\parameters"
AND name LIKE "smb1"
AND data="1";

queries.title: Server Message Block v1 Enabled

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It retrieves MS Office settings from registry:
"VBAWarnings" - this key determines how to run macros:
* 1 - untrusted and trusted macros are allowed to run without notification.
* 2 - all untrusted macros are disabled, but users are notified about untrusted macros and can enable or disable untrusted macros. Trusted macros are allowed to run without notification. This is the default setting.
* 3 - unsigned macros are disabled without notification. Users are notified about signed macros and can enable or disable signed macros. Trusted macros are allowed to run without notification.
* 4 ( Recommended ) - untrusted macros are disabled and users are not notified that untrusted macros are disabled. In addition, users cannot use the Message Bar or any other dialog to enable untrusted macros. Trusted macros are allowed to run without notification.
"PackagerPrompt" - prevents activation of OLE packages:
* 0 - no prompt from Office when user clicks, object executes
* 1 - prompt from Office when user clicks, object executes
* 2 ( Recommended ) - no prompt, object does not execute
"DisableAllActiveX" - disables ActiveX controls
* 1 ( Recommended ) - disabled
* 0 - enabled
"AccessVBOM" - enables VBA macro execution
* 1 - enabled
* 0 ( Recommended ) - disabled

osQuery.label: windows_registry_office_macros
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE path IN (
SELECT path || '\Security\VBAWarnings' FROM registry WHERE key IN (
SELECT path FROM registry WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Microsoft\Office"));

osQuery.label: windows_registry_office_ole
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE path IN (
SELECT path || '\Security\PackagerPrompt' FROM registry WHERE key IN (
SELECT path FROM registry WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Microsoft\Office"));

osQuery.label: windows_registry_office_activex
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE
path LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Microsoft\Office\Common\Security\DisableAllActiveX";

osQuery.label: windows_registry_office_accessvbom
osQuery.name: Registry Data
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, name, data, DATETIME(mtime, "unixepoch") AS last_modified
FROM registry
WHERE path IN (
SELECT path || '\Security\AccessVBOM' FROM registry WHERE key IN (
SELECT path FROM registry WHERE key LIKE "HKEY_USERS\%\Software\Microsoft\Office"));

queries.title: Windows Registry MS Office Settings

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. It checks the hashes of accessibility tools to ensure they do not match the hashes of cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or explorer.exe, which could be used to gain unauthorized access to the system.

osQuery.label: accessibility_features_file_replacement_tmp
osQuery.name: Accessibility Features Files Temp
osQuery.sql:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE accessibility_features_hashes AS 
SELECT hash.path AS accessibility_features_path, hash.directory AS accessibility_features_directory, hash.sha256 AS accessibility_features_sha256 
FROM hash 
WHERE path IN ("C:\Windows\System32\osk.exe","C:\Windows\System32\sethc.exe","C:\Windows\System32\Narrator.exe","C:\Windows\System32\Magnify.exe","C:\Windows\System32\DisplaySwitch.exe");
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE replacements_hashes AS 
SELECT hash.path AS replacement_path, 
hash.directory AS replacement_directory, 
hash.sha256 AS replacement_sha256 
FROM hash 
WHERE path IN ("C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe","C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe","C:\Windows\SysWOW64\explorer.exe");

osQuery.label: accessibility_features_file_replacement
osQuery.name: Accessibility Features Files

queries.title: Accessibility Features File Replacement Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to MacOS. Gatekeeper is a security feature for the MacOS designed to prevent installation of malicious applications, specifically applications that are not signed by Apple or a developer enrolled in Apple"s developer program. This query retrieves from the host the list of applications that have been allowed by a user but would otherwise be blocked by Gatekeeper:
* path - path of executable allowed to run
* requirement - code signing requirement language
* last change time
* last modification time

osQuery.label: macos_gatekeeper_approved_apps_monitoring
osQuery.name: Gatekeeper Approved Applications
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
path, 
requirement, 
datetime(ctime, "unixepoch") as last_changed, 
datetime(mtime, "unixepoch") as last_modified  
FROM gatekeeper_approved_apps
WHERE 
path LIKE regex_match(path, (SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n = "path_pattern"), 0);

queries.title: Gatekeeper Approved Applications Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It accepts a process name and finds all child processes spawned by that process. Data retrieved:
* pid - process id
* name - the process path or shorthand argv[0]
* path - path to executed binary
* cmdline - complete argv
* cwd - process current working directory
* uid - unsigned user id
* gid - unsigned group id
* on_disk - the process path exists yes=1, no=0, unknown=-1
* total_size - total virtual memory size

osQuery.label: process_children_search_tmp
osQuery.name: Process Children Temp
osQuery.sql:
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE parent_child AS 
SELECT child_processes.pid AS child_pid, 
child_processes.name AS child_name, 
parent_processes.pid AS parent_pid, 
parent_processes.name AS parent_name
FROM processes child_processes INNER JOIN processes parent_processes 
ON child_processes.parent=parent_processes.pid;

osQuery.label: process_children_search
osQuery.name: Process Children
osQuery.sql:
SELECT pid, name, path, cmdline, cwd, uid, gid, on_disk, total_size 
FROM processes 
WHERE pid IN 
(SELECT child_pid FROM parent_child WHERE parent_name LIKE 
(SELECT v FROM __vars WHERE n="parent_process"));

queries.title: Process Children Search

queries.description: This query is applicable to Windows. GetForegroundWindow function retrieves a handle to the window with which the user is currently working. It is often used by malware authors to get the list of Windows applications running on the system. This can be used to collect information about the target system or to provide the context for keylogging results.

osQuery.label: powershell_getforegroundwindow_detected
osQuery.name: Powershell GetForegroundWindow Events

queries.title: Powershell GetForegroundWindow Detected

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It identifies unusual child processes of Service Host (svchost.exe) process which normally does not have any child processes:
* process id
* process name
* path to executed binary
* process command line
* SHA256 of executed binary if it is present on disk

osQuery.label: windows_processes_unusual_svchost_children
osQuery.name: Processes
osQuery.sql:
SELECT 
p.pid, p.name, p.path, p.cmdline, h.sha256 
FROM processes p
LEFT JOIN hash h ON h.path = p.path 
WHERE 
p.parent IN (
SELECT p.pid
FROM processes p 
WHERE 
p.name LIKE "%svchost.exe%" AND (
p.cmdline LIKE regex_match(lower(p.cmdline), ".*(wdisystemhost|licensemanager|storsvc|cdpsvc|cdbhsvc|bthavctpsvc|sstpsvc|wdiservicehost|imgsvc|trkwks|wpnservice|ikeext|policyagent).*", 0) OR 
p.cmdline LIKE regex_match(lower(p.cmdline), ".*(cryptsvc|netprofm|profsvc|staterepository|camsvc|lanmanworkstation|nlasvc|eventlog|hidserv|displayenhancementservice|shellhwdetection|apphostsvc|fhsvc|cscservice|pushtoinstall).*", 0))) AND
p.name NOT IN ("csrss.exe", "winlogon.exe", "werfault.exe","werfaultsecure.exe","wermgr.exe");

queries.title: Unusual Svchost.exe Children Monitoring

queries.description:
This query is applicable to Windows. It can detect presence of China Chopper webshell in specific server directories:
* search for china chopper webshell in server host directories
* search for china chopper webshell used by Hafnium

osQuery.label: yara_china_chopper_webshell_1
osQuery.name: yara_china_chopper_webshell_1
osQuery.sql:
SELECT
path, matches, count, strings
FROM yara
WHERE
(path LIKE "C:\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\%" OR
path LIKE "C:\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\%" OR
path LIKE "C:\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\OAB%") AND 
sigrule='rule china_chopper_webshell_1 { meta: author = "FireEye" strings: $s0 = /[<]\%\@\sPage\sLanguage=.jscript.%[>][<]%eval.{1,10}RequestItem.{1,50}unsafe/ nocase ascii $s1 = /http(s)?:\/\/f\/[<]script\sLanguage=.Jscript.\s+runat=.server.[>].*Page_Load.*eval.*Request.+unsafe/ nocase ascii $s3 = /[<]\%\@(\s)?Page\sLanguage=.Jscript.\%[>]\n+[<]\%eval.{1,200}System\.Convert\.FromBase64String.{1,100}char\([0-9]{1,5}.{1,300}\%[>]/ nocase ascii condition: any of them }' AND
matches!=""

queries.title: Windows China Chopper Webshell Monitoring
warnings: This query can take some time to load the results due to YARA scans.

queries.description:
